+ ALBEMARLE CORPORATION [1 Press Release]

+ ALBEMARLE ANNOUNCES PLANS TO IDLE ITS KEMERTON LITHIUM HYDROXIDE PROCESSING PLANT

CHARLOTTE, North Carolina, February 11, 2026 - Albemarle Corporation (NYSE: ALB), a global leader in providing essential elements for mobility, energy, connectivity and health, today announced it will idle the remaining operating train, Train 1, at its Kemerton lithium hydroxide processing plant in Western Australia and place it into care and maintenance effective immediately. Today's announcement follows actions in 2024 to place Train 2 into care and maintenance and to cease expansion plans for Trains 3 and 4.

The Kemerton plant processes spodumene from the Greenbushes mine, one of the world's best spodumene resources. Albemarle holds ownership interest and half of the offtake rights from Greenbushes through an Australian joint venture. Kemerton, with its proven technology and commercial scale lithium hydroxide production, was built to enable the development of a Western lithium supply chain.

"Idling operations at Kemerton was a difficult decision. It follows significant actions we have taken over the past two and a half years to reduce operating costs during an extended period of price volatility in the market," said Kent Masters, Albemarle Chairman and CEO. "Unfortunately, recent lithium price improvements alone are not enough to offset the challenges facing Western hard-rock lithium conversion operations. This decision improves our financial flexibility and preserves optionality."

The decision is expected to be accretive to adjusted EBITDA beginning in the second quarter of 2026 with no impact to projected 2026 volumes. Albemarle will meet customer demand for lithium hydroxide through other production channels.

Albemarle's mining interests in Australia, including its holdings in Greenbushes and Wodgina and its exploration interests in Western Australia, are not impacted by the Kemerton decision as they remain core components of the company's strategy.

In a separate press release issued today, Albemarle shared its fourth quarter and full year 2025 earnings results. The company will provide further details on its conference call to discuss its financial results at 8 a.m. EST on Thursday, February 12, 2026.

Source: Company Website

value.Title: April 28, 2026: JV of Zachry and Traylor Bros. to Replace Roosevelt Bridge in Southeastern Oklahoma

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JGH-XDD3-RRRY-T27B-00000-00April 28, 2026: JV of Zachry and Traylor Bros. to Replace Roosevelt Bridge in Southeastern Oklahoma2026-05-02T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:09:17ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSApril 28, 2026: JV of Zachry and Traylor Bros. to Replace Roosevelt Bridge in Southeastern Oklahoma

Zachry Construction Corporation is entering Oklahoma's market by securing a contract to replace the US-70 Roosevelt Bridge, in a joint venture with Traylor Bros., Inc. The Oklahoma Department of Transportation selected the Zachry-Traylor JV to execute the Roosevelt Bridge replacement, marking a milestone in Zachry's regional expansion.

The announcement, dated 28th April 2026, notes the project will modernize a key transportation corridor linking Bryan and Marshall counties and serving Lake Texoma visitors and local communities.

The new four-lane bridge will provide bicycle and pedestrian accommodations, a median barrier, and upgraded lighting. The project also includes demolishing the existing bridge, with traffic maintained on it during construction to minimize disruption, as crews build the new crossing.

Zachry Construction's long history in heavy and highway work pairs with Traylor Bros.'s experience on complex bridge projects in a joint venture designed to deliver a resilient, safe, and reliable crossing for the region. The completion target is 1st October 2029, reflecting a commitment to lasting value for Oklahoma communities.

SECTION 1 ZACHRY GROUP PROFILE

1.1 ACTIVITIES

Zachry Group, founded in 1924, is a leading construction and engineering firm headquartered in San Antonio, Texas. Employing over 20,000 people, it provides turnkey services in power, energy, chemicals, and industrial sectors, generating over $3 billion annually. Its subsidiaries, like JVIC, offer maintenance and fabrication. In 2024, Zachry expanded renewable energy projects, including solar and wind facilities. Its culture emphasizes safety, citizenship, and employee training, with a commitment to community health and ethical practices. Competing with Fluor, Zachry stands out for its EPC (engineering, procurement, construction) expertise and safety standards. Recent initiatives include digital project management tools and carbon-neutral construction goals, aligning with sustainability trends.

Mission: Excellence in Service

Zachry Group's mission is clear: "To be the best at what we do, delivering distinctive value to our customers." This unwavering commitment drives their pursuit of operational excellence and customer satisfaction.

Vision: The Trusted Leader

The company's vision reflects its aspirations: "To be the most trusted name in industrial infrastructure services." This trust is built on a foundation of consistent performance, integrity, and innovation.

Core Values: Principles That Drive Success

Zachry Group's core values define its culture and approach to business:

Think Long Term: Decisions are made with future generations in mind.

Care About People: The safety, health, and well-being of employees are paramount.

Do the Right Thing: Integrity, honesty, and courage guide all actions.

Honor Commitments: Reliability and trustworthiness are central to their operations.

The Power of "We": Collaboration and teamwork drive success.

Make a Positive Impact: Leaving every project and community better than before.

By adhering to these values, the Zachry Group continues to shape North America's industrial landscape with innovation, trust, and a long-term perspective.

1.2 SUMMARY

Website: https://www.zachrygroup.com/

SECTION 2 ZACHRY GROUP RANKING

Zachry Group was ranked 127 in the Forbes list of Top 216 American Private Companies by Total Revenues in 2024.

urn:contentItem:6JG8-YRR3-RTSP-Y2B9-00000-00ZACHRY GROUP - RECENT NEWS OF BECHTEL CORPORATION AND PEERS2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:14:51ZLexisNexisSTOCKZACHRY GROUP - RECENT NEWS OF BECHTEL CORPORATION AND PEERS

DASHBOARD: ZACHRY GROUP

Websitehttps://www.zachrygroup.com/IndustryConstrustion

ACTIVITIES:

Zachry Group, founded in 1924, is a leading construction and engineering firm headquartered in San Antonio, Texas. Employing over 20,000 people, it provides turnkey services in power, energy, chemicals, and industrial sectors, generating over $3 billion annually. Its subsidiaries, like JVIC, offer maintenance and fabrication. In 2024, Zachry expanded renewable energy projects, including solar and wind facilities. Its culture emphasizes safety, citizenship, and employee training, with a commitment to community health and ethical practices. Competing with Fluor, Zachry stands out for its EPC (engineering, procurement, construction) expertise and safety standards. Recent initiatives include digital project management tools and carbon-neutral construction goals, aligning with sustainability trends.

+ ZACHRY GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ ZACHRY CONSTRUCTION CORPORATION NAMES TRAVIS MROSS AS NEW PRESIDENT

Zachry Construction Corporation has appointed Travis Mross as its new president, a leadership transition aimed at bolstering the company's growth strategy across vital infrastructure markets. This announcement comes as Zachry Construction continues to see strong demand in sectors including transportation, water, and civil sitework. The appointment of Mross, effective immediately, signifies a commitment to operational excellence and client service.Mross's journey with Zachry Construction began in 2000 as an intern, and he has since developed into a key figure within the organization, serving as executive vice president for the past six years. Since the date of his promotion on 21st April 2026, Mross is expected to leverage his extensive experience to enhance the company’s strategic direction and operational capabilities. David Zachry, CEO of Zachry Construction Corporation, expressed confidence in Mross's ability to lead the firm into its next phase of growth.In his new role, Mross will prioritize strategic growth and operational excellence, focusing on enhancing the company's capabilities across transportation, water, underground utility, and civil sitework operations. His leadership is anticipated to strengthen the company’s culture and foster team development, while also maintaining an innovative and results-oriented approach in executing large-scale projects.The need for critical infrastructure continues to escalate, with owners increasingly valuing schedule certainty, transparency, and disciplined execution. To address this demand, Zachry Construction has recently expanded its footprint through the acquisition of Crescent Constructors, enhancing its capabilities in water and wastewater plant infrastructure. Mross emphasized the importance of delivering consistency and high standards across all projects.With over a century of experience in delivering complex infrastructure projects, Zachry Construction Corporation is well-positioned for future growth. The company’s commitment to safety practices, technical expertise, and reliable project management is crucial as it adapts to the evolving needs of the infrastructure market. This leadership change marks a pivotal moment for Zachry Construction as it aims to enhance the communities it serves while navigating the challenges and opportunities within the industry.

+ ZACHRY GROUP UNVEILS NEW BRANDING AS IT TARGETS EXPANDING MARKETS

Zachry Construction Corp. is positioning itself for growth with a refreshed brand identity. The San Antonio-based company announced changes to its logo and tagline, 'Unearth Opportunity,' as it seeks to expand into new markets while remaining true to its South Texas roots. The announcement was made on 4th February 2026.The century-old construction firm is pivoting towards the booming data center sector and enhancing its presence in the water industry. Executive Vice President Travis Mross explained that the rebranding reflects the company's commitment to evolving alongside the industries it serves. Zachry Construction is set to provide essential sitework and underground utilities for data centers, which includes everything from excavation to drainage.In addition to its work in data centers, Zachry Construction is broadening its portfolio in the water sector. The acquisition of Crescent Constructors Inc. in October marks a significant step towards entering the process and treatment side of water infrastructure. Mross noted that the water infrastructure sector is among the fastest-growing segments in construction, and Zachry aims to capitalize on this trend.Zachry Construction has established a robust reputation in both the public and private sectors, having completed over 5,500 projects. Mross emphasized that the new branding aims to communicate the company's mission clearly to all stakeholders, including clients and the public. He stated, 'Just like the industry evolves, society evolves, so does our logo.'Tracing its origins back to H.B. Zachry Co., founded in 1924, the company has transformed under the leadership of the Zachry family. Following a reorganization in 2008, Zachry Construction and Zachry Group emerged as two distinct entities. Today, Zachry Construction continues to build on its legacy while adapting to the changing landscape of the construction industry.

+ ZACHRY GROUP CHOSEN FOR KEY FEED ROLE IN LOW-CARBON METHANOL INITIATIVE

Zachry Group has been selected for the Front-End Engineering Design (FEED) scope of a groundbreaking low-carbon blue methanol project located in Lake Charles, Louisiana. Six days ago, the company announced its selection in a move that positions it at the forefront of sustainable industrial development.The plant aims to capture approximately 1 million tons per year (tpy) of CO2 emissions, setting a new benchmark in methanol production and significantly aiding industrial decarbonization efforts. The ongoing FEED phase will define the technical and economic foundations of the project, paving the way for a final investment decision (FID) expected in the second quarter of 2026.John Zachry, Chairman and CEO of Zachry Group, expressed enthusiasm about the partnership with Lake Charles Methanol II, stating, "We are honoured to partner with Lake Charles Methanol II on this transformative project. This award reinforces our leadership in executing technically advanced, low-carbon solutions at scale." His remarks highlight the company’s commitment to sustainability and innovative industrial practices.As the energy sector increasingly shifts towards decarbonization, Zachry Group’s involvement in this venture underscores its strategic focus on low-carbon technologies. The project is set to contribute significantly to the broader goal of reducing greenhouse gas emissions across the industrial landscape.Investors and industry stakeholders are keenly watching the progress of this initiative, as the successful implementation of the FEED phase could serve as a precedent for future low-carbon projects. With a final investment decision anticipated in early 2026, Zachry Group may find itself in a pivotal role within the evolving landscape of sustainable energy solutions.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ BECHTEL [3 News That Matters]

+ BECHTEL-ENKA CONSORTIUM UNDER PRESSURE TO MEET HIGHWAY DEADLINES IN SERBIA

Serbian President Aleksandar Vu i announced today that he met with representatives of the Bechtel-Enka company, discussing the ongoing construction of a highway that aims to connect multiple cities across Serbia. During this meeting, held on the 21st of April 2026, Vu i emphasized the importance of adhering to the project timelines, particularly for the section stretching from a ak to Kraljevo.Vu i stated, "I am particularly adamant about respecting the deadlines in the a ak-Kraljevo section, which holds significant importance for all of western and southwestern Serbia." This section is part of a larger initiative, the Moravian Corridor, that is seen as one of the most complex infrastructural projects in Serbia's recent history. The government views the timely completion of this highway as crucial for regional development and connectivity.The Bechtel-Enka consortium has faced scrutiny in the past regarding project timelines and budget overruns in various international projects. As Serbia continues to invest heavily in infrastructure development, the pressure is mounting on Bechtel-Enka to deliver as promised. Vu i 's insistence on meeting deadlines reflects the government's broader strategy to enhance economic growth through improved transportation networks.This highway project not only aims to facilitate domestic travel and commerce but also seeks to attract foreign investment by improving Serbia's infrastructure. The government believes that a well-developed transport network will contribute positively to the overall economic landscape of the country, making timely project completion all the more critical.As the Bechtel-Enka consortium moves forward with the construction, stakeholders will be closely monitoring progress to ensure that the timelines set by Vu i are met. The outcome of this project could have significant implications for both the consortium and the Serbian economy as a whole.

+ BECHTEL'S CRAIG ALBERT FUELS NEW CONSTRUCTION PROGRAM AT UNC CHARLOTTE

UNC Charlotte alumni Craig and Darla Albert have made a significant leadership gift to the university, aimed at launching a new model for construction education in one of the nation’s fastest-growing markets. This initiative will be housed within The Albert School of Construction in the William States Lee College of Engineering. The school will emphasize the full capital project lifecycle, preparing students to lead the planning, engineering, and delivery of complex infrastructure projects. This announcement comes on the 7th of April, 2026.Chancellor Sharon L. Gaber highlighted the importance of this investment, stating, "This investment allows us to align education more directly with the needs of our region and the nation." As Charlotte and North Carolina continue to grow, the complexity of infrastructure demands increases, necessitating graduates who are equipped to contribute effectively. Craig Albert, the president and chief operating officer of Bechtel Group, emphasized the pressing need for a skilled workforce to support essential industries, noting a real shortage of construction capacity.The Albert School of Construction aims to build upon the university’s 50 years of construction education by integrating disciplines across engineering, business, and data science into a cohesive academic framework. Rob Keynton, dean of the William States Lee College of Engineering, explained that the program’s unique approach encompasses the entire capital project lifecycle. This comprehensive perspective positions UNC Charlotte as a leader in the evolution of construction education, emphasizing industry partnerships and research strengths.Leading the school will be Executive Director Lingguang Song, who has a background as a professor and department chair in engineering technology and construction management. By offering a curriculum that aligns closely with industry needs, the program aspires to ensure graduates can adapt quickly to professional demands. Craig Albert stated, "My hope is that companies will see graduates from this program climbing the learning curve more quickly. They’ll be equipped for exactly what the industry needs."The Alberts’ leadership gift marks the initial phase of a broader initiative to expand the school’s facilities, programs, and industry collaborations. Future plans include the development of hands-on learning environments, an integrated curriculum with industry input, and endowed faculty positions in critical construction areas. The Alberts are also co-chairs of the For the Love of Charlotte campaign, initiated to raise a record $500 million for the university. Craig Albert expressed a desire for other successful alumni to follow their lead in supporting UNC Charlotte’s growth and impact.

+ BECHTEL APPOINTS LUCY MARTIN AS NEW PRESIDENT OF MINING & METALS DIVISION

Bechtel has announced the appointment of Lucy Martin as president of its Mining & Metals (M&M) business, effective April 6, 2026. This significant leadership change comes as Ailie MacAdam prepares to retire in May after a distinguished 41-year career with the company.Craig Albert, Bechtel President and COO, stated on 3rd April 2026, "Lucy is a proven leader with the strategic vision, deep mining sector knowledge, operational discipline, and customer focus to advance our Mining & Metals business." Martin has spent over two decades at Bechtel, where she has developed high-performing global teams aimed at solving complex challenges for customers across various markets.In her new role, Martin expressed her intent to build on the strong momentum of Bechtel's Mining & Metals division, emphasizing the need to adapt to a rapidly evolving global market. "Our focus will be on enhancing how we deliver results for our customers, particularly in areas such as cost-effective development, productivity, safety, reliability, and certainty of outcomes," she added.MacAdam's tenure saw substantial achievements, including the advancement of major projects in key global markets and the highest new work bookings in 15 years for the Mining & Metals business in 2025. Her leadership has left a lasting impact, characterized by improved financial performance and sustained growth.Bechtel continues to be a trusted partner in engineering, construction, and project management, serving a variety of sectors, including Energy, Infrastructure, Manufacturing & Technology, Mining & Metals, and Nuclear. As it moves forward under new leadership, the company remains committed to delivering successful outcomes for its clients and addressing the demands of a changing energy landscape.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KIEWIT CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ KIEWIT CORPORATION PARTNERS IN $7.1 BILLION AUSTIN LIGHT RAIL PROJECT

A joint venture between Kiewit Building Group and Austin Commercial has secured a significant design-build contract to construct an operations and maintenance facility for the US$7.1 billion Austin Light Rail line. This announcement was made on 28th April 2026, marking a pivotal moment in the development of public transit infrastructure in Austin, Texas.The planned railway system will encompass 15 stations along a 10-mile stretch, featuring all-electric trains expected to run every five to ten minutes throughout most of the day. Kiewit Corporation, in collaboration with local partner Austin Commercial, will oversee the design and construction of the facility where Austin’s light rail vehicles will be stored, serviced, and dispatched, ensuring operational efficiency.In addition to storage and maintenance, the facility will provide a workspace for operators, maintenance crews, and support staff, thereby contributing to the overall functionality of the light rail system. The selected team for this project includes 10 subcontractors focused on the design phase, which is anticipated to generate thousands of jobs during both the design and construction phases.Greg Canally, CEO of the Austin Transit Partnership (ATP), emphasized the importance of this development, stating, "This is another major step forward, and we now have the full construction team that will deliver Austin’s light rail system. The Operations and Maintenance Facility is integral to the success of the system—it’s where trains are maintained, cleaned and prepared for service each day." Canally highlighted that partnering with a world-class construction firm like Kiewit Corporation, which possesses deep local experience, positions the project for long-term success while fostering job creation and economic opportunities across Central Texas.Previously, an Aecom-led consortium named LINC Austin was awarded the role of delivery partner for the first phase of the larger rail project in December 2024. This consortium includes notable firms such as Parsons, STV, Turner & Townsend, Ardmore Roderick, and CAS Consulting, all of which will play a crucial role in the realization of Austin's light rail ambitions.

+ KIEWIT CORPORATION SELECTED FOR EAST AUSTIN LIGHT RAIL PROJECT

A major piece of Austin's long-discussed light rail is finally moving from plans to prep work. On Wednesday, the Austin Transit Partnership signed off on Kiewit Austin Partnership as the design-build contractor for the light rail Operations and Maintenance Facility, clearing the way for pre-construction and inching a central Project Connect component closer to groundbreakers and bulldozers near the airport. The date of this significant development is 21st April 2026.According to Austin Transit Partnership, the ATP board approved Kiewit Austin Partnership—a joint venture of Kiewit Building Group Inc. and Austin Commercial—as the design-build contractor for the Operations and Maintenance Facility. ATP CEO Greg Canally remarked, “This is another major step forward, and we now have the full construction team that will deliver Austin’s light rail system.” The agency's decision allows KAP to immediately launch pre-construction work, including advancing design, starting the permitting process, and preparing the site for future construction.The operations and maintenance facility is slated for a 62-acre site at the southwest edge of the Montopolis neighborhood, according to ATP. The planned footprint, near U.S. Highway 183 and State Highway 71, will house storage and servicing for up to 40 light rail vehicles and will necessitate property acquisitions affecting dozens of existing businesses. Local reporting has highlighted that roughly one-third of the businesses expected to be displaced are concentrated in Airport Commerce Park, raising concerns about relocation and neighborhood impacts.With KAP in place, ATP plans to expedite the Operations and Maintenance Facility design through permitting and site preparation, paving the way for the broader light rail project to advance toward full construction. Industry estimates indicate that mainline construction could commence in 2027, with initial service targeted for the early 2030s. The Federal Transit Administration's combined Final Environmental Impact Statement and Record of Decision, published in January 2026, has cleared the project to enter final design and begin land acquisition.ATP noted that the KAP team already includes around 10 subcontractors during the design phase, with opportunities expected to increase once construction ramps up. The larger Project Connect program is projected to create or support thousands—potentially tens of thousands—of jobs across Texas during design and construction. On the operations side, the maintenance facility is expected to support approximately 300 long-term jobs once the system is operational, further contributing to local economic growth.

+ ATERNIUM ENGAGES KIEWIT FOR HYDROGEN PRODUCTION FACILITY PRE-FEED

Aternium has appointed Kiewit Engineering Group Inc. to undertake the pre-front-end engineering and design (pre-FEED) for its inaugural integrated hydrogen and heavy water production facility. This strategic move comes as Aternium aims to advance its ambitious $1 billion plan to produce clean hydrogen and critical heavy water domestically. The announcement was made on 26th March 2026, signaling a significant step in the company's broader, multi-plant development strategy focused on establishing a robust infrastructure for advanced energy materials.The planned facility will be located in the Mid-Atlantic region of the United States, designed to meet the growing demand for low-carbon energy solutions. Aternium's initiative not only seeks to enhance domestic capabilities in the production of heavy water—currently sourced primarily from countries like China and Iran—but also aims to address critical supply chain vulnerabilities. Andrew Cottone, Founder and CEO of Aternium, emphasized the importance of this collaboration with Kiewit, citing shared values in technical expertise and safety standards.According to Cottone, the initiative is poised to create significant socioeconomic benefits by fostering workforce development and job creation in local communities. Aternium's focus on integrating advanced technologies and innovative processes is expected to stimulate regional economic growth, thereby supporting the transition to cleaner energy systems. Kiewit will play a pivotal role in executing Aternium's unique business model, which aims to deliver both clean hydrogen and heavy water at competitive costs.Kiewit’s involvement in the project brings extensive experience in engineering, procurement, and construction within the energy sector. Tyler Nordquist, Executive Vice President at Kiewit Engineering Group Inc., expressed confidence in the partnership, highlighting the company's ability to add substantial value during the pre-FEED phase. This phase will include defining the project's scope, optimizing design parameters, and laying a solid foundation for future development.Aternium's integrated production approach is noteworthy as it combines clean hydrogen generation with a patent-pending process for isolating heavy water. This dual-output model not only aims to reduce hydrogen production costs but also addresses a critical vulnerability in the U.S. supply of strategic materials. With the increasing demand for clean energy and high-tech components, Aternium's initiative is strategically positioned at the intersection of energy transition and advanced manufacturing, thus offering a scalable solution that aligns with both economic and technological priorities.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION [3 News That Matters]

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION APPOINTS JASON PAGE TO LEAD NASHVILLE SPECIAL SERVICES GROUP

DPR Construction has announced the appointment of Jason Page as the new lead for its Special Services Group in Nashville. As of 1st April 2026, Page brings over 14 years of experience to the role, having joined DPR Construction in January 2012. His career has been marked by a versatile portfolio, encompassing projects from minor renovations to large-scale, complex developments.Since relocating to Nashville in April 2021, Jason Page has successfully spearheaded numerous corporate interior and entertainment projects, consistently delivering high-quality results under tight deadlines. His approach emphasizes a client-first leadership style, which has allowed him to cultivate strong relationships across various market sectors in the region. Page's passion for renovation and tenant improvement work has positioned him as a key player in DPR Construction's local operations.The DPR Special Services Group (SSG) plays a pivotal role in executing a diverse range of projects, from targeted improvements like parking garage stair installations to expansive interior buildouts for new office and entertainment spaces. Under Page's leadership, the SSG is expected to enhance its capabilities in meeting the evolving needs of clients and stakeholders in Nashville.In addition to project leadership, Jason Page has been actively involved in mentoring and coaching project teams within DPR Construction. He has shared best practices for executing quick-turn, high-impact work, fostering a culture of collaboration and innovation. His dedication to developing talent within the organization is seen as a valuable asset as DPR Construction continues to expand its footprint in Nashville.With Jason Page at the helm of the Special Services Group, DPR Construction is well-positioned to leverage his expertise and leadership as it navigates the competitive landscape of Nashville's construction market. The company's commitment to delivering exceptional service and quality projects remains steadfast, supported by Page's extensive experience and strategic vision.

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION ACHIEVES GREAT PLACE TO WORK® CERTIFICATION AMID HIGH EMPLOYEE SATISFACTION

DPR Construction, a leader in the technical building sector, has secured Great Place To Work® Certification, with an impressive 97 percent of its employees affirming that it is a great place to work. This remarkable statistic is notably 40 points higher than the typical score given to workplaces across the United States. The certification, awarded on 31st March 2026, underscores DPR's commitment to fostering a workplace culture characterized by pride, belonging, and opportunity.George Pfeffer, CEO of DPR Construction, emphasized the significance of this certification, which reflects the sentiments of the employees who embody the company's culture. He stated, "Being Certified(TradeMark) as a great place to work is especially meaningful because it comes directly from our people, who bring our culture to life." Pfeffer highlighted the company's focus on creating an inclusive environment where employees feel trusted, supported, and encouraged to grow, thus enabling them to perform at their best.DPR Construction's operational philosophy is built on empowering employees across its various projects to make decisions and share information openly. This approach encourages initiative, whether it involves innovating on complex tasks, stepping into new roles, or contributing ideas beyond formal job descriptions. Employees benefit from career paths aligned with their aspirations, supported by various learning and development programs.The research conducted by Great Place To Work® corroborates DPR's employee-centric strategy, revealing that a significant portion of the workforce feels a sense of pride in their contributions and a strong sense of belonging within the company. The certification further enhances DPR's reputation as an employer of choice, with additional accolades from U.S. News & World Report and Newsweek recognizing it as one of the best companies to work for, particularly in over 15 U.S. cities over the past year.DPR Construction has built a strong legacy since its founding in 1990, evolving into a multi-billion-dollar organization that specializes in complex and sustainable projects across various sectors. Its commitment to innovation, through the use of virtual design and construction and self-performing craft, positions DPR among the top contractors in the industry, employing over 11,000 professionals worldwide. For more details about DPR's culture and career opportunities, visit their official website.

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION PIONEERS URBAN DEVELOPMENT IN SOUTH FLORIDA

DPR Construction Pioneers Urban Development in South Florida. The rapid transformation of SouthFlorida's skyline is being propelled by a powerful combination of collaboration, innovation, and strategic planning, with DPR Construction playing a central role in shaping the region's future. On the 28th of March 2026, during an industry roundtable hosted in partnership with the South Florida Business Journal, leaders from various sectors discussed how coordinated efforts are redefining construction and development across the region.Collaboration Reshaping Project Delivery. Industry experts underscored that cross-sector partnerships have shifted from being optional to essential for effectively navigating the complexities of modern urban development. By aligning stakeholders early in the process, projects are achieving measurable enhancements in efficiency, safety, and overall quality. This approach is particularly vital in South Florida, where limited land availability, rising costs, and increasing regulatory demands necessitate more integrated solutions. Successful developments must transcend mere functionality, aiming to create long-term community value through thoughtful design, sustainability, and resilience.Innovation and Technology Driving Efficiency. Innovation is a critical driver in accelerating project timelines and improving outcomes. DPR Construction and its partners are harnessing advanced building techniques such as prefabrication and modular construction, alongside emerging technologies like artificial intelligence, to streamline workflows and eliminate inefficiencies. These tools are enabling construction teams to improve accuracy, reduce material waste, enhance job site safety, and shorten delivery timelines. The integration of digital tools and data-driven decision-making further facilitates better forecasting and coordination across complex, multi-phase projects.Addressing Workforce and Growth Challenges. The robust population and economic growth in South Florida have highlighted the pressing demand for skilled labor. Industry leaders emphasized the importance of workforce development initiatives, including collaborations with educational institutions and training programs aimed at attracting and retaining talent within the construction sector. With numerous projects underway and increased investment flowing into the region, maintaining a steady pipeline of skilled workers is essential for sustaining growth momentum.Building for Long-Term Resilience. The discussion also shifted towards the significance of constructing resilient communities capable of withstanding future challenges, including climate impacts and fluctuating economic conditions. Developers and contractors are prioritizing sustainable building practices, energy efficiency, climate-resilient infrastructure, and community-centered design in their projects. These initiatives aim to ensure that new developments remain valuable assets for decades. As South Florida

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ PARSONS CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ PARSONS CORPORATION EXPANDS ROLE IN NEW YORK'S INFRASTRUCTURE DEVELOPMENTS

Parsons Corporation (NYSE:PSN) has been selected to manage the construction of the Newtown Creek Combined Sewer Overflow Storage Tunnel in New York City. This announcement comes on the heels of a significant contract awarded on the 14th of April 2026, which highlights the company's commitment to innovative infrastructure solutions. As part of this project, Parsons Corporation will oversee the development of a large-diameter storage tunnel and a dewatering pump station aimed at protecting local waterways from untreated discharges.The New York City Department of Environmental Protection (DEP) has entrusted Parsons Corporation with this critical task, recognizing the firm's extensive experience in managing over 250 tunnel and ocean outfall projects worldwide. This infrastructure initiative is not only intended to modernize New York City's physical assets but also to enhance public health and improve water quality throughout the region. By leveraging its technology-driven approach, Parsons Corporation aims to deliver effective solutions that are essential for urban sustainability.In addition to its work in New York, Parsons Corporation has further solidified its position in the defense sector. A joint venture with GSI Americas has secured a $25 million contract with the United States Coast Guard (USCG). This agreement focuses on providing program management services that are vital to the USCG’s Execution Plan, which includes significant upgrades to shore infrastructure and the implementation of transformational technologies.The partnership with GSI Americas underscores Parsons Corporation's strategic intent to support federal agencies in securing and modernizing critical digital and physical assets. This multifaceted approach not only enhances the operational capabilities of the USCG but also aligns with the company's broader objectives in the defense and cybersecurity markets.Overall, Parsons Corporation's recent contracts in both infrastructure and defense sectors demonstrate its versatility and commitment to delivering integrated solutions that meet the evolving needs of urban environments and national security. As the firm continues to expand its portfolio, investors may find interest in its potential for future growth and innovation in these key areas.

+ PARSONS TEAMS WITH GSIA FOR $25 MILLION US COAST GUARD CONTRACT

CHANTILLY, Va. — Parsons Corporation (NYSE: PSN) announced today that its joint venture with GSI Americas Inc. (GSIA) has been awarded a contract valued at $25 million by the United States Coast Guard (USCG) for program management services. This contract, granted on 15th April 2026, includes a one-year base period of performance with four additional one-year option periods.The scope of this contract entails providing program management services that will facilitate the USCG’s ambitious Force Design 2028 Execution Plan, which represents the largest capital investment in the history of the Coast Guard. This comprehensive initiative aims to enhance USCG capabilities through significant upgrades to assets, technology, and shore infrastructure, supported by a total budget of $24.5 billion allocated for modernizing platforms and systems, improving training facilities, and boosting mission-critical operational capacities.Martin Boson, president of Engineered Systems for Parsons, expressed enthusiasm for the partnership, stating, "We look forward to leveraging our team’s combined global program management experience and project delivery success to support the U.S. Coast Guard in this historic modernization effort that strengthens readiness and builds a more agile, capable, and responsive force." As a leader in program management, Parsons brings a wealth of expertise and integrated services essential for the successful execution of this significant plan.GSIA, known for its innovative and sustainable energy solutions, will also play a pivotal role in this joint venture. Michael Warminsky, senior vice president for GSIA, commented, "We are proud to support the U.S. Coast Guard’s Force Design 2028 Execution Plan alongside Parsons, contributing to a program that enhances operational readiness, modernizes critical infrastructure, and strengthens the nation’s maritime security posture."With a strong track record in managing complex projects, Parsons is recognized as the top program management services firm by Engineering News-Record. The company provides comprehensive support throughout the project lifecycle, including master planning, design, construction management, and asset management. This partnership with GSIA further underscores Parsons' commitment to delivering impactful solutions in national security and infrastructure.

+ PARSONS CORPORATION WINS $409M ROOSEVELT BRIDGE DESIGN PROJECT

Parsons Corporation (NYSE:PSN) has been selected as the lead designer for the Roosevelt Bridge Replacement Project, a significant infrastructure initiative valued at $409 million. The announcement was made on 14th April 2026, detailing the project’s aim to replace an aging crossing over Lake Texoma in Bryan and Marshall Counties, Oklahoma.The Oklahoma Department of Transportation has awarded the construction contract to the Zachry Construction/Traylor Brothers Construction Joint Venture, while Parsons will handle the design aspects of the new bridge. This structure will span approximately two miles over one of the largest reservoirs in the United States, which is managed by the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers. The design will include 63 spans, with substructure elements extending about 150 feet below the lake surface.To facilitate ongoing traffic flow during construction, the project will be executed in two phases. The original Roosevelt Bridge, constructed in 1945, serves as a vital connector for local residents, freight, and travelers. The replacement initiative has secured Oklahoma’s largest federal grant amounting to $124 million in 2024, complemented by state funding.Despite the recent contract win, Parsons’ stock has experienced a decline of 35% over the past six months, currently trading at $56.01, with a market capitalization of approximately $6 billion. Analysts have indicated that the stock may be undervalued relative to its fair value, positioning it as a potential opportunity for investors. The company’s president of Infrastructure North America, Mark Fialkowski, emphasized Parsons’ legacy in bridge design, stating that this project exemplifies their commitment to delivering complex infrastructure solutions worldwide.In addition to the Roosevelt Bridge project, Parsons has been active in various significant initiatives, including a 10-year contract with the Utah Department of Transportation for a Vehicle-to-Everything Maintenance and Management System. Furthermore, Parsons is leading the design for a $389 million project on Interstate 64 in Virginia and has been appointed as the project management consultant for the Al Ittihad Sports Village in Saudi Arabia. Despite these advancements, Baird recently downgraded Parsons’ stock rating to Neutral, citing concerns over potential revenue declines in the Middle East, illustrating the complex landscape the company navigates in its growth trajectory.

value.Title: April 28, 2026: Zachry Group Expands into Oklahoma with Roosevelt Bridge Replacement Project

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JGG-S9J3-S0N4-0409-00000-00April 28, 2026: Zachry Group Expands into Oklahoma with Roosevelt Bridge Replacement Project2026-05-02T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:09:17ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSApril 28, 2026: Zachry Group Expands into Oklahoma with Roosevelt Bridge Replacement Project

Zachry Construction Corporation has won a contract to replace the US-70 Roosevelt Bridge in Oklahoma, working in a joint venture with Traylor Bros., Inc. The Oklahoma Department of Transportation selected the Zachry-Traylor JV as the delivery partner, in what is the first collaboration between Zachry Construction and the OklahomaDOT.

The announcement, dated 28th April 2026, underscores a significant upgrade to a corridor that connects Bryan and Marshall counties and serves travelers headed to Lake Texoma.

The project will replace the aging structure with a new four-lane crossing featuring modern amenities including bicycle and pedestrian accommodations, a median barrier, and upgraded lighting. The existing bridge will be demolished, with traffic maintained on the current structure during construction to minimize disruption.

Zachry Construction brings more than a century of experience in heavy and highway work, and the Traylor Bros. team is recognized for its expertise in bridge projects. Together they aim to deliver a state-of-the-art crossing that enhances connectivity and supports regional growth. Completion is targeted for 1st October 2029.

INDEX

SECTION 1 ZACHRY GROUP PROFILE

SECTION 2 ZACHRY GROUP RANKING

SECTION 1 ZACHRY GROUP PROFILE

1.1 ACTIVITIES

Zachry Group, founded in 1924, is a leading construction and engineering firm headquartered in San Antonio, Texas. Employing over 20,000 people, it provides turnkey services in power, energy, chemicals, and industrial sectors, generating over $3 billion annually. Its subsidiaries, like JVIC, offer maintenance and fabrication. In 2024, Zachry expanded renewable energy projects, including solar and wind facilities. Its culture emphasizes safety, citizenship, and employee training, with a commitment to community health and ethical practices. Competing with Fluor, Zachry stands out for its EPC (engineering, procurement, construction) expertise and safety standards. Recent initiatives include digital project management tools and carbon-neutral construction goals, aligning with sustainability trends.

Mission: Excellence in Service

Zachry Group's mission is clear: "To be the best at what we do, delivering distinctive value to our customers." This unwavering commitment drives their pursuit of operational excellence and customer satisfaction.

Vision: The Trusted Leader

The company's vision reflects its aspirations: "To be the most trusted name in industrial infrastructure services." This trust is built on a foundation of consistent performance, integrity, and innovation.

Core Values: Principles That Drive Success

Zachry Group's core values define its culture and approach to business:

Think Long Term: Decisions are made with future generations in mind.

Care About People: The safety, health, and well-being of employees are paramount.

Do the Right Thing: Integrity, honesty, and courage guide all actions.

Honor Commitments: Reliability and trustworthiness are central to their operations.

The Power of "We": Collaboration and teamwork drive success.

Make a Positive Impact: Leaving every project and community better than before.

By adhering to these values, the Zachry Group continues to shape North America's industrial landscape with innovation, trust, and a long-term perspective.

1.2 SUMMARY

Website: https://www.zachrygroup.com/

SECTION 2 ZACHRY GROUP RANKING

Zachry Group was ranked 127 in the Forbes list of Top 216 American Private Companies by Total Revenues in 2024.

urn:contentItem:6JGP-7S63-S121-S545-00000-00AXENS S.A. - INDUSTRY UPDATES AND PEER TRENDS2026-05-02T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:14:51ZLexisNexisSTOCKAXENS S.A. - INDUSTRY UPDATES AND PEER TRENDS

DASHBOARD: AXENS S.A.

PermID4296138916Websitehttp://www.axens.netIndustryChemicals & Energy Processing

ACTIVITIES:

Axens S.A. engages in developing, manufacturing, and selling catalysts and adsorbents. It focuses on the conversion of oil, coal, natural gas, and biomass to clean fuels; the production and purification of petrochemical intermediates. The company offers guard beds and grading materials. It also provides technology licensing and engineering design; and consulting and software services, such as advanced control, simulators, technical, and economic studies to optimize refining units; and process licensing and performance programs. In addition, the company offers operations support for technical assistance, proprietary equipment, training, and catalyst management; and technical assistance, training, and consulting services. It serves oil refining, petrochemicals/chemicals, gases, alternative fuels, and water markets. The company was founded in 2001 and is based in Rueil-Malmaison, France with additional offices in Princeton, New Jersey; and Houston, Texas. It has manufacturing sites in Savannah, Georgia; Calvert City, Kentucky; and Willow Island, West Virginia. It has operations worldwide. Axens S.A. operates as a subsidiary of IFP Energies nouvelles.

+ AXENS S.A. [2 Press Releases]

+ PRAJ DEMONSTRATES INTEGRATED ETHANOL-TO-JET PATHWAY FOR SUSTAINABLE AVIATION FUEL WITH AXENS JETANOL(TRADEMARK) TECHNOLOGY

30 January, 2026 - Praj Industries, a global leader in bioenergy, technology and sustainable solutions, has successfully demonstrated its Ethanol-to-Jet (EtJ) process utilizing Axens JetanolTM technology at its fully Integrated Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF) demonstration plant at Praj Matrix, its R&D centre-marking a significant milestone in the global transition to low-carbon aviation fuels. The announcement was made by Mr. Atul Mulay, President - Bionergy at Praj Industries at Wings India 2026, India's premier aviation industry platform in the presence of industry leaders, policymakers, and key stakeholders from the global aviation ecosystem.

The Integrated SAF demo plant, earlier inaugurated by the Hon'ble Union Minister for Petroleum and Natural Gas, Shri. Hardeep Singh Puri, has now proven its technological readiness for both AtJ pathways. After successful demonstration on bio isobutanol as the feedstock, the plant has now validated bio-ethanol as a feedstock under the EtJ pathway. With this achievement, Praj has further strengthened the versatility and robustness of its feedstock to SAF technology portfolio.

Source: Company Website

+ AXENS CONTINUES ITS PUBLIC CONSULTATION REGARDING ITS CATHODE ACTIVE MATERIAL PRODUCTION PLANT PROJECT

Following the preliminary consultation held from May 5 to July 5, 2025, and the publication of the project owners' report, Axens and RTE (Reseau de Transport d'Electricite - the French Transmission System Operator) have entered into a continuous consultation process to share project-related information with stakeholders and residents of the municipalities concerned within the Valenciennes Metropole and Portes du Hainaut urban communities.

The continuous consultation mechanism implemented by Axens aims to maintain a transparent and constructive dialogue around the project with residents of the municipalities concerned within the Valenciennes Metropole and Portes du Hainaut communities. This is achieved through the reopening of the participatory pages on the consultation website (www.concertation-macaron.fr). The process will make it possible to share and publicly release the results of the various studies and includes the creation of a project monitoring committee composed of stakeholders who expressed particular interest in the project during the preliminary consultation phase.

The National Commission for Public Debate has appointed Ms. Catherine JACQUART as the guarantor of the continuous consultation. The first meeting of the monitoring committee was held on December 16 at the Valenciennes Chamber of Commerce and Industry. It made it possible to present the continuous consultation mechanism as well as several project updates resulting from progress in ongoing studies. The site footprint and layout were further detailed. Technical parameters were also refined: the plant's production capacity is now estimated at 39-43 kt/year, and information related to water consumption as well as the selected cooling system was presented.

Source: Company Website

+ BASF SE [3 Press Releases]

+ BASF: INTERNATIONAL COLOR DAY: WHEN AUTOMOTIVE COLOR MEETS PERFORMANCE, PROTECTION AND SUSTAINABILITY

Color significantly influences consumer purchasing decisions, vehicle longevity and brand identity Creating, protecting and preserving automotive colors combines emotional design and materials science BASF Coatings develops more than 1,300 color concepts every year Automotive color is where emotional design meets materials science. It influences brand identity, purchase decisions, safety perception and even sustainability. In anticipation of International Color Day on March 21, BASF Coatings is highlighting the crucial role color plays in automotive design, manufacturing and repair. Far beyond aesthetics, automotive color is a complex, data-driven, and technically precise discipline that requires profound knowledge substrate pre-treatment, the development of different layers that build the final coating and the skill to exactly replicate the status quo of a color shade even years after its application.

Protecting color from the very first layer

With decades of expertise in color innovation, BASF Coatings is well-positioned to create, protect and preserve automotive color across the full lifecycle of a vehicle. When car owners talk about the color of their car, they usually refer to the visible, colorful basecoat only. But to make a color shine, it takes way more than just the pigmentation. BASF Coatings' integrated approach ensures that vehicles also adhere to stringent durability and sustainability standards such as corrosion protection.

"Pretreatment is the invisible enabler behind durable, sustainable automotive coatings. By offering tailored pretreatment solutions for modern automotive substrates, we create the chemical foundation for corrosion resistance and robust paint adhesion. This first process step is decisive for the long-term performance, durability and sustainability of the entire coating system and the basis to make every color shine," said Meike Flock, Head of Global Segment Management Automotive OEM Surface Treatment.

Designing automotive colors for performance and with responsible material choices

The diversity of today's automotive colors is driven by the precise interplay of pigments and effect materials that give each basecoat its distinctive character. At BASF Coatings, this expertise is combined with close collaboration between the global Color Design team and automotive manufacturers, enabling innovative color solutions that meet high performance standards while allowing the integration of more responsible material choices where feasible.

This approach has recently been recognized by leading design and technology institutions. The trend color REVERENCE DARKNESS of BASF Coatings received the MATERIALICA Design and Technology Award 2025, honoring its use of recycled tire materials as a carbon black source. In parallel, BASF Coatings was nominated as a finalist for the German Ecodesign Award 2025 with its project "Colors with Renewable Pigments", demonstrating how biobased

+ BASF AGRICULTURAL SOLUTIONS INVESTS 17 MILLION EURO TO EXPAND ITS CANOLA BREEDING CENTRE OF INNOVATION IN SASKATOON, CANADA

Strategic investment, building on 30 years of global leadership in canola Construction taking place from spring 2026 through end of 2027, adding breeding capacity and advanced automation Facility will enable faster genetic gain and new trait integration, driving greater yields, disease protection and performance for growers BASF Agricultural Solutions is investing 17 million euro to expand and transform its Canola Breeding Centre of Innovation in Saskatoon, Canada, with construction commencing this spring. The investment will strengthen BASF's position as a global leader in canola advancement and reinforce its long-term commitment to deliver leading-edge genetics for canola growers globally.

For 30 years, InVigor® hybrid canola has played a central role in advancing productivity and performance for canola and oilseed rape growers around the world. This investment enhances BASF's breeding capacities, integrating advanced automation, and accelerating the development of new hybrids.

"Canola is a strategic crop for our Agricultural Solutions business, playing a crucial role for Canadian farmers and supporting food production worldwide," said Livio Tedeschi, President, BASF Agricultural Solutions. "This investment underscores our commitment to advancing our entire portfolio of canola. As the global leader in canola production and innovation, BASF is proud to invest in its future innovation capabilities." Accelerating innovation through advanced breeding technologies

A key component of the Saskatoon facility expansion is the development of new infrastructure to support precision-controlled environment growth systems and high throughput automation pipelines. "These enhancements are critical to implementing genomic selection at scale, enabling faster, more precise breeding decisions and accelerating genetic gain across all InVigor programs. By increasing breeding throughput and improving predictive capabilities, BASF can shorten innovation cycles and deliver improved hybrids to the market more efficiently", explained Doreen Schachtschabel, Senior Vice President, R&D for Seeds & Traits.

The expansion will also include a new advanced research-grade glasshouse designed to support the advancement of future hybrid breeding programs. By expanding its research capabilities in Canada, BASF is strengthening its ability to deliver differentiated solutions that can drive greater yields, improve disease protection and offer overall enhanced performance for farmers.

+ BASF INCREASES PRICES FOR NEOPENTYL GLYCOL IN EUROPE

With immediate effect, or as existing contracts permit, BASF is increasing the prices of the polyalcohol neopentyl glycol (Neol®) in Europe:

Product :Neopentyl glycol

Price Increase + 350 Euro (MT)

Neopentyl glycol is a high-quality intermediate used, for example, to produce polyester resins for coatings, unsaturated polyester resins, lubricants and plasticizers.

+ HONEYWELL INTERNATIONAL INC. [2 Press Releases]

+ TUSKEGEE UNIVERSITY UPGRADES CAMPUS SAFETY SYSTEMS WITH HONEYWELL AI ENABLED SMART ACCESS TECHNOLOGY

CHARLOTTE, N.C., Nov. 18, 2025 -- Tuskegee University, one of the nation's most respected Historically Black Colleges and Universities (HBCUs), today announced a multi-year, strategic project to implement a fully integrated security system across its campus using Honeywell's (NASDAQ: HON) AI-enabled smart access technology. The upgrades will aim to further strengthen Tuskegee University's commitment to the safety of its students, faculty, staff and visitors.

Tuskegee University will utilize Honeywell's state-of-the-art commercial security solutions to deploy centralized access control and real-time monitoring tools, helping improve incident response times and reduce operational complexity for security teams. As higher education institutions nationwide face mounting pressure to enhance security and adapt to new technologies, these upgrades aim to position the University as a leader in on-campus safety.

Tuskegee University will leverage several of Honeywell's Building Automation solutions. Key features include:

Source: Company Website

+ HONEYWELL AND TOTALENERGIES PILOT AI-ASSISTED CONTROL ROOM TO ACCELERATE SHIFT TO INDUSTRIAL AUTONOMY

HOUSTON, Nov. 11, 2025 /PRNewswire/ -- Honeywell (NASDAQ: HON) today announced a collaboration with TotalEnergies for the ongoing pilot of its AI-assisted Experion Operations Assistant at TotalEnergies' Port Arthur Refinery in Texas. The initiative aims to support and empower operators to make timely and informed decisions while also providing the opportunity to enhance operational autonomy.

Built on Honeywell's flagship distributed control system, Experion Operations Assistant is an advanced AI-powered solution designed to transform the way operators monitor plant operations from the control room. By merging operational analytics with real real-time predictive insights, the solution facilitates a more efficient workflow within critical refinery operations. With the integration of this new solution, operators in the control room can forecast potential maintenance events before they happen and minimize risks associated with unsafe operations and production losses.

"Partnering with Honeywell at our Port Arthur Refinery represents an important step in our journey toward operational excellence across our facility," said Raphael Duflos, VP and General Manager of TotalEnergies' Port Arthur Platform. "We believe this solution could contribute to safer operations, reduced downtime, and minimized product losses."

TotalEnergies has already implemented an initial pilot of Experion Operations Assistant at the Port Arthur site's Delayed Coking Unit (DCU). Preliminary results show the AI-assisted solution has successfully forecasted five potential events, helping to minimize downtime and reduce emissions from flaring. The predictions were made an average of 12 minutes in advance of an alarm incident, enabling operators to quickly implement corrective actions before an event.

"Honeywell's decades of domain expertise and industry knowledge are helping to solve our customers' toughest challenges with tangible solutions like Experion Operations Assistant," said Jim Masso, President and CEO of Honeywell Process Solutions. "This pilot with TotalEnergies will mark a meaningful milestone for bridging the gap between autonomous technology and the operators that keep these facilities running safely and efficiently every day."

Located in Southeast Texas near the Gulf Coast, TotalEnergies' Port Arthur Platform refines crude oil into transportation fuels and produces petrochemicals used in a wide range of products like plastics, rubber, and pharmaceuticals. The pilot is a successful collaboration between Honeywell, TotalEnergies' Port Arthur platform and its technology headquarter branch OneTech.

Source: Company Website

+ ALBEMARLE CORPORATION [1 Press Release]

+ ALBEMARLE ANNOUNCES PLANS TO IDLE ITS KEMERTON LITHIUM HYDROXIDE PROCESSING PLANT

CHARLOTTE, North Carolina, February 11, 2026 - Albemarle Corporation (NYSE: ALB), a global leader in providing essential elements for mobility, energy, connectivity and health, today announced it will idle the remaining operating train, Train 1, at its Kemerton lithium hydroxide processing plant in Western Australia and place it into care and maintenance effective immediately. Today's announcement follows actions in 2024 to place Train 2 into care and maintenance and to cease expansion plans for Trains 3 and 4.

The Kemerton plant processes spodumene from the Greenbushes mine, one of the world's best spodumene resources. Albemarle holds ownership interest and half of the offtake rights from Greenbushes through an Australian joint venture. Kemerton, with its proven technology and commercial scale lithium hydroxide production, was built to enable the development of a Western lithium supply chain.

"Idling operations at Kemerton was a difficult decision. It follows significant actions we have taken over the past two and a half years to reduce operating costs during an extended period of price volatility in the market," said Kent Masters, Albemarle Chairman and CEO. "Unfortunately, recent lithium price improvements alone are not enough to offset the challenges facing Western hard-rock lithium conversion operations. This decision improves our financial flexibility and preserves optionality."

The decision is expected to be accretive to adjusted EBITDA beginning in the second quarter of 2026 with no impact to projected 2026 volumes. Albemarle will meet customer demand for lithium hydroxide through other production channels.

Albemarle's mining interests in Australia, including its holdings in Greenbushes and Wodgina and its exploration interests in Western Australia, are not impacted by the Kemerton decision as they remain core components of the company's strategy.

In a separate press release issued today, Albemarle shared its fourth quarter and full year 2025 earnings results. The company will provide further details on its conference call to discuss its financial results at 8 a.m. EST on Thursday, February 12, 2026.

Source: Company Website

value.Title: April 28, 2026: Zachry Group Secures Roosevelt Bridge Replacement in Oklahoma in JV with Traylor Bros.

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JGJ-X4R3-RT7Y-904C-00000-00April 28, 2026: Zachry Group Secures Roosevelt Bridge Replacement in Oklahoma in JV with Traylor Bros.2026-05-02T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:09:17ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSApril 28, 2026: Zachry Group Secures Roosevelt Bridge Replacement in Oklahoma in JV with Traylor Bros.

Zachry Construction Corporation is expanding into Oklahoma after securing the US-70 Roosevelt Bridge replacement contract. The Oklahoma Department of Transportation selected a joint venture of Zachry Group and Traylor Bros., Inc. to deliver the project, marking the first collaboration between Zachry and the Oklahoma DOT as the company seeks to broaden its regional footprint.

The announcement dated 28th April 2026 signals a major upgrade to a corridor connecting Bryan and Marshall counties and serving travelers to the Lake Texoma area. The new four-lane bridge will include bicycle and pedestrian accommodations, a median barrier, and upgraded lighting, while the existing bridge will be demolished with traffic maintained on it during construction to minimize disruption.

Zachry Construction and Traylor Bros. bring extensive experience in heavy and highway construction to the project. The joint venture is designed to deliver a modern crossing that preserves mobility throughout the build and strengthens regional connectivity for the Lake Texoma communities. The completion target is 1st October 2029.

INDEX

SECTION 1 ZACHRY GROUP PROFILE

SECTION 2 ZACHRY GROUP RANKING

SECTION 1 ZACHRY GROUP PROFILE

1.1 ACTIVITIES

Zachry Group, founded in 1924, is a leading construction and engineering firm headquartered in San Antonio, Texas. Employing over 20,000 people, it provides turnkey services in power, energy, chemicals, and industrial sectors, generating over $3 billion annually. Its subsidiaries, like JVIC, offer maintenance and fabrication. In 2024, Zachry expanded renewable energy projects, including solar and wind facilities. Its culture emphasizes safety, citizenship, and employee training, with a commitment to community health and ethical practices. Competing with Fluor, Zachry stands out for its EPC (engineering, procurement, construction) expertise and safety standards. Recent initiatives include digital project management tools and carbon-neutral construction goals, aligning with sustainability trends.

Mission: Excellence in Service

Zachry Group's mission is clear: "To be the best at what we do, delivering distinctive value to our customers." This unwavering commitment drives their pursuit of operational excellence and customer satisfaction.

Vision: The Trusted Leader

The company's vision reflects its aspirations: "To be the most trusted name in industrial infrastructure services." This trust is built on a foundation of consistent performance, integrity, and innovation.

Core Values: Principles That Drive Success

Zachry Group's core values define its culture and approach to business:

Think Long Term: Decisions are made with future generations in mind.

Care About People: The safety, health, and well-being of employees are paramount.

Do the Right Thing: Integrity, honesty, and courage guide all actions.

Honor Commitments: Reliability and trustworthiness are central to their operations.

The Power of "We": Collaboration and teamwork drive success.

Make a Positive Impact: Leaving every project and community better than before.

By adhering to these values, the Zachry Group continues to shape North America's industrial landscape with innovation, trust, and a long-term perspective.

1.2 SUMMARY

Website: https://www.zachrygroup.com/

urn:contentItem:6JGN-XMH3-RTF8-S13T-00000-00April 28, 2026: Zachry Construction Joins Forces with Traylor Bros. for US-70 Roosevelt Bridge Upgrade in Oklahoma2026-05-02T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:09:17ZLexisNexisANNOUNCEMENTSApril 28, 2026: Zachry Construction Joins Forces with Traylor Bros. for US-70 Roosevelt Bridge Upgrade in Oklahoma

Zachry Construction Corporation, in a joint venture with Traylor Bros., Inc., has secured a contract to replace the US-70 Roosevelt Bridge in Oklahoma, marking a strategic expansion into the state. The Oklahoma Department of Transportation selected the Zachry-Traylor JV to lead the project, the first collaboration between Zachry Construction and the OklahomaDOT.

The announcement, made on 28th April 2026, highlights a major upgrade to a corridor connecting Bryan and Marshall counties and serving LakeTexoma travelers. The existing Roosevelt Bridge has operated since 1945 and remains a critical link for local communities and businesses.

The plan calls for a four-lane crossing with bicycle and pedestrian accommodations, a median barrier, and upgraded lighting, with construction staged to demolish the existing bridge while keeping traffic on the bridge during the work to minimize disruption.

The project team combines Zachry's long history in heavy and highway construction with Traylor Bros.' proficiency in complex bridge projects, positioning the teams to deliver a modern crossing that maintains mobility during construction and supports regional growth.

Completion is targeted for 1st October 2029, reflecting a broader effort to bolster safety, reliability, and resilience in the region and to support local commerce and tourism.

ACTIVITIES:

Zachry Group, founded in 1924, is a leading construction and engineering firm headquartered in San Antonio, Texas. Employing over 20,000 people, it provides turnkey services in power, energy, chemicals, and industrial sectors, generating over $3 billion annually. Its subsidiaries, like JVIC, offer maintenance and fabrication. In 2024, Zachry expanded renewable energy projects, including solar and wind facilities. Its culture emphasizes safety, citizenship, and employee training, with a commitment to community health and ethical practices. Competing with Fluor, Zachry stands out for its EPC (engineering, procurement, construction) expertise and safety standards. Recent initiatives include digital project management tools and carbon-neutral construction goals, aligning with sustainability trends.

+ ZACHRY GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ ZACHRY CONSTRUCTION CORPORATION NAMES TRAVIS MROSS AS NEW PRESIDENT

Zachry Construction Corporation has appointed Travis Mross as its new president, a leadership transition aimed at bolstering the company's growth strategy across vital infrastructure markets. This announcement comes as Zachry Construction continues to see strong demand in sectors including transportation, water, and civil sitework. The appointment of Mross, effective immediately, signifies a commitment to operational excellence and client service.Mross's journey with Zachry Construction began in 2000 as an intern, and he has since developed into a key figure within the organization, serving as executive vice president for the past six years. Since the date of his promotion on 21st April 2026, Mross is expected to leverage his extensive experience to enhance the company’s strategic direction and operational capabilities. David Zachry, CEO of Zachry Construction Corporation, expressed confidence in Mross's ability to lead the firm into its next phase of growth.In his new role, Mross will prioritize strategic growth and operational excellence, focusing on enhancing the company's capabilities across transportation, water, underground utility, and civil sitework operations. His leadership is anticipated to strengthen the company’s culture and foster team development, while also maintaining an innovative and results-oriented approach in executing large-scale projects.The need for critical infrastructure continues to escalate, with owners increasingly valuing schedule certainty, transparency, and disciplined execution. To address this demand, Zachry Construction has recently expanded its footprint through the acquisition of Crescent Constructors, enhancing its capabilities in water and wastewater plant infrastructure. Mross emphasized the importance of delivering consistency and high standards across all projects.With over a century of experience in delivering complex infrastructure projects, Zachry Construction Corporation is well-positioned for future growth. The company’s commitment to safety practices, technical expertise, and reliable project management is crucial as it adapts to the evolving needs of the infrastructure market. This leadership change marks a pivotal moment for Zachry Construction as it aims to enhance the communities it serves while navigating the challenges and opportunities within the industry.

+ ZACHRY GROUP UNVEILS NEW BRANDING AS IT TARGETS EXPANDING MARKETS

Zachry Construction Corp. is positioning itself for growth with a refreshed brand identity. The San Antonio-based company announced changes to its logo and tagline, 'Unearth Opportunity,' as it seeks to expand into new markets while remaining true to its South Texas roots. The announcement was made on 4th February 2026.The century-old construction firm is pivoting towards the booming data center sector and enhancing its presence in the water industry. Executive Vice President Travis Mross explained that the rebranding reflects the company's commitment to evolving alongside the industries it serves. Zachry Construction is set to provide essential sitework and underground utilities for data centers, which includes everything from excavation to drainage.In addition to its work in data centers, Zachry Construction is broadening its portfolio in the water sector. The acquisition of Crescent Constructors Inc. in October marks a significant step towards entering the process and treatment side of water infrastructure. Mross noted that the water infrastructure sector is among the fastest-growing segments in construction, and Zachry aims to capitalize on this trend.Zachry Construction has established a robust reputation in both the public and private sectors, having completed over 5,500 projects. Mross emphasized that the new branding aims to communicate the company's mission clearly to all stakeholders, including clients and the public. He stated, 'Just like the industry evolves, society evolves, so does our logo.'Tracing its origins back to H.B. Zachry Co., founded in 1924, the company has transformed under the leadership of the Zachry family. Following a reorganization in 2008, Zachry Construction and Zachry Group emerged as two distinct entities. Today, Zachry Construction continues to build on its legacy while adapting to the changing landscape of the construction industry.

+ ZACHRY GROUP CHOSEN FOR KEY FEED ROLE IN LOW-CARBON METHANOL INITIATIVE

Zachry Group has been selected for the Front-End Engineering Design (FEED) scope of a groundbreaking low-carbon blue methanol project located in Lake Charles, Louisiana. Six days ago, the company announced its selection in a move that positions it at the forefront of sustainable industrial development.The plant aims to capture approximately 1 million tons per year (tpy) of CO2 emissions, setting a new benchmark in methanol production and significantly aiding industrial decarbonization efforts. The ongoing FEED phase will define the technical and economic foundations of the project, paving the way for a final investment decision (FID) expected in the second quarter of 2026.John Zachry, Chairman and CEO of Zachry Group, expressed enthusiasm about the partnership with Lake Charles Methanol II, stating, "We are honoured to partner with Lake Charles Methanol II on this transformative project. This award reinforces our leadership in executing technically advanced, low-carbon solutions at scale." His remarks highlight the company’s commitment to sustainability and innovative industrial practices.As the energy sector increasingly shifts towards decarbonization, Zachry Group’s involvement in this venture underscores its strategic focus on low-carbon technologies. The project is set to contribute significantly to the broader goal of reducing greenhouse gas emissions across the industrial landscape.Investors and industry stakeholders are keenly watching the progress of this initiative, as the successful implementation of the FEED phase could serve as a precedent for future low-carbon projects. With a final investment decision anticipated in early 2026, Zachry Group may find itself in a pivotal role within the evolving landscape of sustainable energy solutions.
