Collaboration brings together global EPC expertise and local construction capabilities to support complex project delivery Bechtel, a global leader in engineering, procurement, construction, and project management, today announced an agreement with Echeverria Izquierdo Montajes Industriales S.A. (EIMISA), a leading Chilean construction and industrial services company, to support the delivery of select large-scale mining and infrastructure projects in Chile and across South America.

As mining investment accelerates across the region, customers are placing greater emphasis on how complex projects are delivered - seeking efficient execution while maintaining high standards for safety and quality.

Under the agreement, Bechtel and EIMISA will work together on projects where their capabilities are most complementary, combining Bechtel's expertise in engineering, procurement, and project delivery with EIMISA's proven track record in direct-hire construction across Chile.

Source: Company Website

+ BECHTEL AND NABTU PARTNER TO ADVANCE NUCLEAR CONSTRUCTION APPRENTICESHIPS

Bechtel and North America's Building Trades Unions (NABTU) today announced a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) to advance and modernize apprenticeship programs supporting the construction of a new generation of U.S. nuclear power plants.

The agreement reflects a strategic alignment between one of the world's most experienced builders of nuclear infrastructure and North America's largest federation of skilled construction unions. Together, the organizations will strengthen apprenticeship pathways and expand specialized training to develop the craft expertise and quality standards required to deliver nuclear projects.

Through this partnership, Bechtel and NABTU will work together to ensure apprenticeship programs continue to evolve to meet the scale of resources needed as well as the technical requirements of modern nuclear construction.

Source: Company Website

+ KIEWIT CORPORATION [3 Press Releases]

+ KIEWIT CORPORATION PROTECTING WHAT LIES BELOW

Near the base of Nch'kay', or Mount Garibaldi, the Squamish community, in western Canada, has lived for decades with the threat of large debris flows. These fast-moving surges of mud, water, trees and boulders can cascade down the river with little warning. The Cheekye River, whose name comes from the Skwxwu7mesh (Squamish Nation language) word for "muddy waters," has a long history of these events.

Because of the historical risk, development on the alluvial fan - the broad deposit of sediment formed where the river spreads out at the base of the mountain - had been restricted for years. Despite many existing homes and businesses on the fan being in harm's way, little had been done to reduce the risk. Sqomish Sea to Sky Developments, a partnership between the Squamish Nation and local land development company Matthews West, owns just over 500 acres of this land.

Source: Company Website

+ KIEWIT'S 85 YEARS IN CANADA

In 2026, Kiewit proudly marks its 85th year of building in Canada. The company's Canadian story began in 1941 in the North, supporting construction of the Norman Wells Oil Refinery and Pipeline. In 1949, Kiewit opened its first Canadian office in Vancouver, establishing a permanent presence in the country. From those early roots, the company expanded across regions and markets, building the relationships, workforce and operational capacity required to serve clients coast to coast to coast. For the past 85 years, Kiewit has delivered complex projects across transportation, energy, marine, water, industrial and emerging sectors that support a changing economy.

"Diversifying our markets has made us a stronger, more resilient business," said Ryan Tones, president of Peter Kiewit Sons ULC. "Our teams have built hydroelectric facilities, ports and marine infrastructure, highways and transit systems, pipelines and power facilities, and essential water and wastewater systems that protect communities and natural resources. Our investment in our Marine Yard on the West Coast and our continued growth in water, wastewater and mining, reflect our commitment to serving Canada's evolving infrastructure needs and creating long-term opportunities for our people and partners."

Today, Kiewit employs more than 4,000 people across Canada, including a strong and growing craft workforce. Positioned across the country, these teams are building on decades of experience while preparing for Canada's next chapter.

The following timeline highlights select milestones and projects from the past 85 years and illustrates Kiewit's enduring role in advancing Canada's energy, infrastructure and economic development.

+ PARSONS CORPORATION PARSONS MARKS GROUNDBREAKING OF SR 400 EXPRESS LANES PROJECT IN GEORGIA

CHANTILLY, Va., April 27, 2026 -- Parsons Corporation (NYSE: PSN) joined the Georgia Department of Transportation (GDOT), the State Road and Tollway Authority (SRTA), project partners, and community leaders to mark the groundbreaking of the State Route 400 (SR 400) Express Lanes Project on April 22. SR 400 is the largest transportation infrastructure project in Georgias history and the largest transportation public-private partnership (P3) project in the United States.

Parsons serves as the lead designer on the SR 400 Peach Partners team, supporting delivery of the approximately $4.6 billion project. The project will add new, dynamically priced express lanes along a 16-mile corridor from the North Springs MARTA station in Fulton County to McFarland Parkway in Forsyth County, improving mobility and the regional economy, enhancing travel reliability, and supporting future transit opportunities in one of the regions most heavily traveled corridors.

This project represents a significant investment in the future of transportation in Georgia, said Mark Fialkowski, president of Infrastructure North America, Parsons Corporation. Parsons brings deep experience delivering complex, large-scale infrastructure programs, and we are proud to support this transformative project that will improve mobility, strengthen regional connectivity, and support long-term economic growth.

The SR 400 Express Lanes Project is a key component of Georgias Major Mobility Investment Program and is designed to address increasing congestion while providing reliable travel options for commuters and freight. The project will also enable future multimodal improvements, including transit enhancements along the corridor.

Parsons has supported critical infrastructure programs across Georgia for decades, providing engineering, design, and program management services that help modernize transportation systems and connect communities across the state. The companys role on the SR 400 project reflects its continued commitment to delivering innovative, resilient infrastructure solutions that meet the evolving needs of its clients.

Parsons has more than half a century of experience designing, delivering, and protecting the infrastructure that connects our communities around the world, including roads and highways; bridges; passenger and freight rail; public transit; airports; and ports and waterways. Our experience includes more than 10,000 miles of roadways, 4,500 bridges, and more than 50 advanced traffic management system deployments that help cities and states improve safety and travel efficiency while also reducing emissions and energy costs to enhance the quality of life in the communities we serve.

To learn more about Parsons road and highway expertise, visit www.Parsons.com/road-highway/.

+ PARSONS CORPORATION PARSONS DEMAND RESPONSE TECHNOLOGY TO OPTIMIZE AND ENABLE LOS ANGELES DEPARTMENT OF WATER & POWERS OPERATIONS AND STRATEGIC PLAN

CHANTILLY, Va., April 20, 2026 -- Parsons Corporation (NYSE: PSN) announced today that the company has been selected by the Los Angeles Department of Water & Power (LADWP) to provide demand response technology, integration, and services to help manage and optimize the operation of distributed energy sources within the electrical grid.

Utility providers around the world are reshaping their capacity plans to meet the growing needs driven by the boom of artificial intelligence and data centers, said Mark Fialkowski, president, Infrastructure North America for Parsons. We take great pride in helping cities around the world deliver resilient energy systems for their populations and look forward to applying our innovative technology to enhance LADWPs demand response and renewable electricity capacity.

Under the contract, Parsons will provide the Demand Response Management System (DRMS) to LADWP, which will serve as key infrastructure in the utilitys drive to achieve 100 percent clean electricity by 2035 or sooner, while helping expand LADWPs demand response capacity by more than fourfold under the five-year contract. Unlike traditional, siloed DRMS solutions, the Parsons system offers out-of-the-box interoperability in critical areas including meter data management (MDM), customer care, billing, Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition (SCADA) and automated distribution management systems (ADMS).

Parsons DRMS helps enhance grid resiliency by securely interfacing distributed energy resources with existing utility systems and legacy fuel supplies. By enabling seamless data exchange and coordination across DRMS, MDM, SCADA, ADMS, and other operational platforms, it empowers grid operators with improved situational awareness, control, and resiliency. Parsons has implemented meter and operational management integration solutions with more than 400 public power utilities across the United States.

To learn more about Parsons comprehensive solutions for distributed energy resource management, visit https://www.parsons.com/products/derms/.

+ PARSONS CORPORATION PARSONS SELECTED AS CONSTRUCTION MANAGER FOR MAJOR NEW YORK CITY TUNNEL PROGRAM

CHANTILLY, Va., April 14, 2026 -- Parsons Corporation (NYSE: PSN) announced today that the company has been selected by the New York City Department of Environmental Protection (DEP) to provide construction management services for the Newtown Creek Combined Sewer Overflow Storage Tunnel project in New York City. Parsons will serve as a member of Newtown Creek CSO Partners, a joint venture with AECOM and EPC Consultants Inc., supporting the delivery of a major underground infrastructure program designed to eliminate untreated sewer discharges into local waterways.

The 16-year program represents one of New York Citys most significant wastewater infrastructure investments, addressing longstanding environmental challenges across Brooklyn and Queens. Parsons will provide construction management (CM) services for the large-diameter storage tunnel, tunnel dewatering pump station, and other structures, drawing on its experience delivering complex tunnel and sewer programs in dense urban environments.

Parsons brings decades of experience delivering complex tunnel and water infrastructure programs that improve environmental outcomes and strengthen community resilience, said Mark Fialkowski, president, Infrastructure North America for Parsons. We are proud to support New York City on this critical project that will reduce sewer overflows, enhance water quality, and help meet important regulatory commitments.

The project, which includes approximately 3.25 miles of tunnel and related facilities, is critical to improving water quality and protecting public health in surrounding communities. Once complete, the system will capture and store excess combined sewer flows during storm events, reducing environmental impacts and supporting long-term sustainability goals. As the construction manager, Parsons will utilize the DEPs Construction Management Information System (CMIS), which the company originally helped develop and deploy, to support efficient project delivery, coordination, and oversight.

Parsons is a global leader in critical infrastructure excellence, with more than 80 years of experience planning, designing, and overseeing the construction of complex tunnel projects throughout the world, including the Rondout-West Branch Bypass Tunnel, Kensico-Eastview Connection Tunnel, and Catskill Aqueduct Repair and Rehabilitation projects; each of these signature NYCDEP projects, in which Parsons provided comprehensive CM services. With more than 250 tunnels and ocean outfalls around the world, Parsons projects span the full range of challenging geologic environments, from seismically active SouthernCalifornia to the Chesapeake Bays alluvial coastal plain to the mixed-face and hard-rock conditions found in and around New York City.

To learn more about Parsons water and wastewater expertise, visit https://www.parsons.com/markets/water-wastewater/.

urn:contentItem:6JK4-CP13-RS50-G236-00000-00Tesla, Inc. (NASDAQ:TSLA) Posts Baron Capital's Ongoing Investment in Tesla and SpaceX2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:14:51ZLexisNexisTesla, Inc. (NASDAQ:TSLA) Posts Baron Capital's Ongoing Investment in Tesla and SpaceX

AMERICAN DAILY STOCK REPORT

UNITED STATES EDITION

13 May 2026 14:15 EDT

On May 12, 2026, Ron Baron appeared on CNBC's Squawk Box to reaffirm Baron Capital's commitment to investing in Tesla, Inc. (NASDAQ:TSLA) and SpaceX, noting that the firm will continue to acquire shares in both companies. Baron, who manages about $55 billion in assets, described SpaceX as Baron Capital's largest holding after the stake rose from $1.7 billion in 2017 to more than $15 billion, while Tesla sits around $5 billion in the portfolio.

Baron outlined SpaceX's mid-2026 IPO plan and disclosed plans to purchase an additional $1 billion of SpaceX shares at the offering. He predicted SpaceX could become the largest company globally, driven by projects like Starlink and reusable rockets. He is optimistic about Tesla as well, projecting the stock could trade between $2,000 and $2,500 per share within a decade, implying a market capitalization near $8.3 trillion. He stressed that he does not intend to sell Tesla or SpaceX shares during his lifetime, aligning with a long-term, patient approach

IN TODAY'S REPORT:

SECTION 1 TESLA ACTIVITIES

SECTION 1 Tesla Activities

Activities

Tesla, Inc., a leading American electric vehicle and clean energy company headquartered in Austin, Texas, and listed on the Nasdaq, focuses on designing, manufacturing, and selling innovative electric cars, energy storage systems, and solar products. Its core business revolves around accelerating the global transition to sustainable energy, with flagship electric vehicles like the Model 3 and Model Y dominating the market through advanced battery technology and performance features. The fastest-growing segments include energy storage solutions, such as the Powerwall and Megapack, which are increasingly adopted for residential, commercial, and utility-scale applications to support renewable energy integration and grid stability. Additionally, Tesla's autonomous driving technology and artificial intelligence capabilities, embedded in vehicles and expanded through its Full Self-Driving software, are experiencing rapid expansion, driven by rising demand for smart mobility solutions. This diversification, coupled with global manufacturing expansions in China and Germany, positions Tesla as a key player in the evolving sustainable technology landscape. It is the NASDAQ's largest Auto company by market capitalisation.

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JK1-XSF3-SHYX-40NH-00000-00ZACHRY GROUP - UPDATES FROM BECHTEL CORPORATION AND PEERS: RECENT INSIGHTS2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:09:33ZLexisNexisSTOCKZACHRY GROUP - UPDATES FROM BECHTEL CORPORATION AND PEERS: RECENT INSIGHTS

DASHBOARD: ZACHRY GROUP

Websitehttps://www.zachrygroup.com/IndustryConstrustion

ACTIVITIES:

Zachry Group, founded in 1924, is a leading construction and engineering firm headquartered in San Antonio, Texas. Employing over 20,000 people, it provides turnkey services in power, energy, chemicals, and industrial sectors, generating over $3 billion annually. Its subsidiaries, like JVIC, offer maintenance and fabrication. In 2024, Zachry expanded renewable energy projects, including solar and wind facilities. Its culture emphasizes safety, citizenship, and employee training, with a commitment to community health and ethical practices. Competing with Fluor, Zachry stands out for its EPC (engineering, procurement, construction) expertise and safety standards. Recent initiatives include digital project management tools and carbon-neutral construction goals, aligning with sustainability trends.

+ ZACHRY GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ ZACHRY GROUP EXPANDS INTO OKLAHOMA WITH ROOSEVELT BRIDGE REPLACEMENT PROJECT

Zachry Construction Corporation has won a contract to replace the US-70 Roosevelt Bridge in Oklahoma, working in a joint venture with Traylor Bros., Inc. The Oklahoma Department of Transportation selected the Zachry-Traylor JV as the delivery partner, in what is the first collaboration between Zachry Construction and the OklahomaDOT.The announcement, dated 28th April 2026, underscores a significant upgrade to a corridor that connects Bryan and Marshall counties and serves travelers headed to Lake Texoma.The project will replace the aging structure with a new four-lane crossing featuring modern amenities including bicycle and pedestrian accommodations, a median barrier, and upgraded lighting. The existing bridge will be demolished, with traffic maintained on the current structure during construction to minimize disruption.Zachry Construction brings more than a century of experience in heavy and highway work, and the Traylor Bros. team is recognized for its expertise in bridge projects. Together they aim to deliver a state-of-the-art crossing that enhances connectivity and supports regional growth. Completion is targeted for 1st October 2029.

+ ZACHRY CONSTRUCTION PROMOTES MROSS TO PRESIDENT AS WATER AND INFRASTRUCTURE GROWTH ACCELERATES

Travis Mross has been elevated to president at Zachry Construction Corp., with the transition effective 29th April 2026. The promotion signals a continuing development plan for the San Antonio-based, family-owned contractor.Beginning as an intern in 2000 and rising to executive vice president for six years, Mross helped guide the firm's strategy and improve efficiency. He takes over from David Zachry, who will devote his efforts to CEO responsibilities.Zachry Construction remains a leader in heavy civil, roadway and infrastructure work, having completed more than 6,000 projects across public and private sectors. The company is expanding in water and wastewater and recently acquired Crescent Constructors Inc. to broaden its capabilities in that area.As president, Mross will oversee water, transportation, underground utilities and civil sitework, prioritizing growth, execution discipline and a strong corporate culture. He stated, "Our job is to bring consistency, discipline, and a high standard of execution to every project, which will guide how we serve clients and grow the business."

+ ZACHRY GROUP SECURES GROUND LEASE, ADDS PARKING LOTS TO SUPPORT MONARCH HOTEL

On Apr. 29, 2026, Zachry Group, through its affiliate Zachry Park Garage QOZB LLC, leased a parking lot from St. John's Evangelical Lutheran Church for a site across South Presa Street from the church near La Villita and Hemisfair, and acquired a smaller lot from St. Johns Four Square LLC, bringing the combined area to about 1.2 acres. Those parcels were part of a larger development plan by Dennis McDaniel in partnership with Opportunity Home San Antonio for 252 apartments and retail space, a plan that stalled during the pandemic.A Zachry representative said the spaces will support operational needs for the Monarch San Antonio hotel, which opened overlooking Civic Park. There are no immediate plans for additional development on the sites.Zachry Group, founded in 1924 by Henry Bartell 'Pat' Zachry, has a long-standing presence in San Antonio and was involved in the Monarch project, a roughly $185 million development that has helped shape the city’s hospitality landscape.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ BECHTEL [3 News That Matters]

+ NABTU AND BECHTEL PARTNER TO DEVELOP THE NEXT-GENERATION NUCLEAR WORKFORCE

Bechtel and NABTU have signed an MOU to modernize apprenticeship programs for nuclear construction, as the United States pursues growth in nuclear energy including SMRs and large reactors, with the agreement signed on May 5, 2026. The agreement outlines steps to identify specialized craft skills, align curricula with industry requirements, and modernize training centers to reflect new construction methods. Leaders from Bechtel and NABTU stressed the importance of a robust training pipeline for the next generation of projects, citing Vogtle Units 3 and 4 and the Natrium Demonstration Project as benchmarks.

+ BECHTEL, EIMISA AGREE ON PARTNERSHIP TO ENHANCE REGIONAL MINING AND INFRASTRUCTURE DELIVERY

A global leader in engineering and construction has formed a strategic partnership with Echeverria Izquierdo Montajes Industriales S.A. (EIMISA), a prominent Chilean firm specializing in construction and industrial services. The announcement was made on 6th May 2026. The collaboration is designed to support the delivery of mining and infrastructure projects across Chile and South America.The partnership will leverage the strengths of both companies as mining investments surge, delivering projects that meet stringent safety and quality standards. The engineering and project management expertise of the global firm will complement EIMISA's direct-hire construction throughout Chile.Dario Barros Izquierdo, CEO of EIMISA, noted that the agreement builds on a foundation of trust between the two companies, adding, "Bechtel's global expertise and technical resources, combined with our local knowledge and execution capability, create a powerful team to support Chile's growing portfolio of complex projects."

+ SOWELA AND BECHTEL ROLL OUT FREE PIPEFITTING PROGRAM TO BOOST LOCAL WORKFORCE

Bechtel and SOWELA Technical Community College are partnering to offer a free Pipefitting Training Program at SOWELA's Main Campus in Lake Charles, aimed at preparing locals for careers in pipefitting across manufacturing, energy, and building systems. The program runs 26th May 2026 through 13th November 2026.The curriculum covers core job duties, safety principles, and the tools and materials used in the trade, with a strong emphasis on hands-on work installing and repairing high- and low-pressure piping systems.Classes run weekdays from 8 a.m. to 3 p.m. Upon completion, participants earn OSHA 10-hour credential and are positioned to pursue NCCER Pipefitting certifications, boosting their prospects in a crowded market.A background check and drug screening are required for employment consideration. Details for registration are at www.sowela.edu/pipefitting, and inquiries can be directed to SOWELA's Office of Workforce Solutions at (337) 421-6560 or workforce@sowela.edu

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KIEWIT CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ STRATEGIC JV SECURES MAJOR O &M CENTER FOR AUSTIN LIGHT RAIL AS PART OF $7.1B PROJECT

This announcement was made on 28th April 2026. A joint venture of Kiewit Building Group and Austin Commercial has moved forward with a design-build contract to construct an operations and maintenance facility for the US$7.1 billion Austin Light Rail line. The project will support a 10-mile corridor with 15 stations and all-electric trains planned to operate every five to ten minutes.The O&M facility will store, service, and dispatch trains and provide workspace for operators, maintenance crews, and support staff.Ten design-phase subcontractors are involved, and the overall effort is expected to generate thousands of jobs during both the design and construction phases. ATP Chief Executive Greg Canally noted the project as a major step forward and highlighted the value of a locally experienced partner like Kiewit.Earlier, Aecom-led LINC Austin was named the delivery partner for the first phase, with a roster that includes Parsons, STV, Turner & Townsend, Ardmore Roderick, and CAS Consulting.

+ KAP JV CHOSEN TO LEAD LIGHT RAIL O&M FACILITY AS PROJECT CONNECT ADVANCES

The Austin Transit Partnership revealed that Kiewit Austin Partnership (KAP), a Kiewit Building Group Inc. and Austin Commercial joint venture, will design and build the Operations and Maintenance Facility, accelerating pre-construction activities. The development was announced on 21st April 2026.The 62-acre OMF site will sit on the southwest edge of the Montopolis neighborhood, near U.S. 183 and SH 71, and will store and service up to 40 light rail vehicles. Property acquisitions tied to the project will affect dozens of existing businesses, with Airport Commerce Park highlighted as an area of relocation concern.KAP’s design team already includes about 10 subcontractors, with expectations for expanded involvement as construction ramps up. The broader Project Connect program is expected to generate substantial job activity across Texas during design and construction, while the OMF itself is projected to support around 300 long-term jobs once in service. Mainline construction could begin in 2027, with service anticipated for the early 2030s, following the Federal Transit Administration's January 2026 EIS/ROD clearance for final design and land acquisition.

+ STRATEGIC PARTNERSHIP ADVANCES U.S. CLEAN HYDROGEN AND HEAVY WATER PROJECT TO PRE-FEED

Aternium has selected Kiewit Engineering Group Inc. to conduct the pre-front-end engineering and design for its first integrated hydrogen and heavy water facility, aligning with the company's plan to deploy a $1 billion program to produce these materials domestically. The announcement was made on 26th March 2026.The Mid-Atlantic site will address demand for low-carbon energy and bolster U.S. capabilities in heavy water, which is currently sourced largely from foreign suppliers. Andrew Cottone noted that the collaboration with Kiewit reflects a shared commitment to technical excellence and safety.The project combines clean hydrogen production with a patent-pending heavy water isolation process, aiming to reduce hydrogen costs and strengthen domestic supply. The pre-FEED will set the scope and design foundations for future development, positioning the venture at the crossroads of energy transition and advanced manufacturing.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION [3 News That Matters]

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION ANNOUNCES 9,100-SF INNSBROOK EXPANSION UNDER COUNTY INCENTIVES

DPR Construction plans to expand its Innsbrook office by 9,100 square feet, backed by incentives from Henrico County. The date of this announcement is 29th April 2026.The expansion is designed to accommodate a growing workforce and enhance operations in the region.Officials say the project reflects confidence in the local market and will create additional job opportunities while reinforcing the firm’s sustainability commitments.The design will feature modern elements and sustainable practices, aligning with DPR’s mission to lead in sustainable construction.Completion is anticipated within the next year.

+ DPR TAPS JASON PAGE TO LEAD NASHVILLE SPECIAL SERVICES GROUP AND EXPAND INTERIOR BUILDOUTS

The SSG handles a range of work from parking garage stair installations to interior buildouts, and under Page's leadership the group aims to meet evolving client and stakeholder needs while Page mentors teams and promotes best practices for quick-turn, high-impact work.

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION ACHIEVES GREAT PLACE TO WORK CERTIFICATION WITH 97% POSITIVE EMPLOYEE FEEDBACK

DPR Construction has earned Great Place To Work Certification after 97 percent of its employees affirmed it is a great place to work, a rate about 40 points above the national average. The certification, awarded on 31st March 2026, highlights a culture built on pride, belonging and opportunity.According to CEO George Pfeffer, the certification reflects the real sentiments of the people who carry the company’s culture. "Being Certified(TradeMark) as a great place to work is especially meaningful because it comes directly from our people, who bring our culture to life," he said, underscoring an emphasis on trust, inclusion, and growth.DPR’s approach centers on empowering employees to make decisions and share information openly across projects. This openness supports initiative, whether in innovating on complex tasks, taking on new roles, or offering ideas outside formal job descriptions, with learning and development programs backing career progression.The recognition complements DPR’s broader reputation as an employer of choice and its ongoing achievements, including industry accolades and expansion across markets. DPR, founded in 1990, has grown into a multi billion dollar contractor employing over 11,000 professionals worldwide, with innovations such as virtual design and construction and self performing craft.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ PARSONS CORPORATION [3 News That Matters]

+ PARSONS CORPORATION (NYSE:PSN) DEPLOYS SBIR-BACKED AUTONOMOUS DRONE INITIATIVE WITH EVERYWHERE COMMUNICATIONS

Parsons Corporation (NYSE:PSN) and EVERYWHERE Communications established a strategic partnership to push autonomous drone technology under SBIR, focusing on operations in disconnected environments. The partnership was confirmed on 4th of May 2026. By leveraging Iridium Satellite technology, EVERYWHERE Communications introduces a resilient data transport layer that enables drones to operate autonomously while transmitting sensor data back to operators and command systems. The platform supports beyond-line-of-sight operations, reliable data exfiltration and autonomous mission execution, with scalable coordination for multiple drones on low-bandwidth links. Jake Bailey, President of EVERYWHERE Communications, states, "In austere environments, connectivity is never guaranteed. We're enabling drones to keep operating—and keep delivering intelligence—even when the network is compromised." Parsons contributes TAK-as-a-Service (TaaS) to provide scalable, mission-ready integration and sustainment services tailored for the Department of War and federal agencies, enhancing real-time situational awareness and data interoperability. Mike Kushin, President of Defense and Intelligence at Parsons, notes, "In an era where every second and every signal matters, this collaboration brings together resilient autonomy in the air and trusted mission systems on the ground to give our warfighters and intelligence professionals a decisive information advantage." The parties aim to redefine operational capabilities for mission-critical teams worldwide.

+ PARSONS CORPORATION (NYSE: PSN) REPORTS COLLABORATION WITH THE BORING COMPANY ON DUBAI LOOP DESIGN VERIFICATION AND PERMITTING

CHANTILLY, Va. — The Boring Company awards Parsons a nine-month contract for the Dubai Loop project, dated 5th May 2026Parsons will act as TBC’s delegated program manager, delivering Independent Design Verification, Stakeholder Management, and Permitting and NOC Support for the pilot phase, and conducting multidisciplinary reviews across civil, structural, mechanical, electrical, safety, and utility disciplinesPierre Santoni, president of Infrastructure EMEA at Parsons, said, "Parsons has proudly played a central role in developing some of the most important and prestigious critical infrastructure programs across the UAE since our first programs there in the 1980s." The partnership with TBC aligns with the UAE leadership's Dubai 2040 Urban Master Plan and aims to advance local capabilities through collaborationThe Dubai Loop project, in collaboration with Dubai’s Roads and Transit Authority (RTA), seeks to expand urban mobility through tunnels and stations. Parsons has worked with the RTA since 2005, contributing to key projects such as the Dubai Metro and various highway and bridge programsParsons has managed more than 250 tunneling projects globally and has extensive road and highway experience covering over 10,000 miles on six continents

+ PARSONS CORPORATION (NYSE:PSN) EXPANDS NYC INFRASTRUCTURE ROLE WITH NEWTOWN CREEK TUNNEL CONTRACT AND USCG JV PROGRAM MANAGEMENT

Parsons Corporation (NYSE:PSN) Selected To Manage The Newtown Creek CSO Storage Tunnel For NYC DEP Following A Contract Award On Apr. 14, 2026. The Project Calls For A Large-Diameter Storage Tunnel And A Dewatering Pump Station To Protect Local WaterwaysThe New York City Department Of Environmental Protection Cites Parsons Experience On More Than 250 Tunnel And Ocean Outfall Projects Worldwide. The Initiative Aims To Modernize Municipal Assets And Improve Water Quality Through A Technology-Driven ApproachParsons Also Forms A Joint Venture With GSI Americas To Secure A US Coast Guard Program Management Contract Worth $25 Million. The Agreement Supports The USCG Execution Plan, Including Shore Infrastructure Upgrades And Deployment Of Transformational Technologies

urn:contentItem:6JJN-93V3-RRRC-30R3-00000-00ZACHRY GROUP - RECENT UPDATES FROM BECHTEL CORPORATION AND OTHER PEERS2026-05-12T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:14:51ZLexisNexisSTOCKZACHRY GROUP - RECENT UPDATES FROM BECHTEL CORPORATION AND OTHER PEERS

DASHBOARD: ZACHRY GROUP

Websitehttps://www.zachrygroup.com/IndustryConstrustion

ACTIVITIES:

Zachry Group, founded in 1924, is a leading construction and engineering firm headquartered in San Antonio, Texas. Employing over 20,000 people, it provides turnkey services in power, energy, chemicals, and industrial sectors, generating over $3 billion annually. Its subsidiaries, like JVIC, offer maintenance and fabrication. In 2024, Zachry expanded renewable energy projects, including solar and wind facilities. Its culture emphasizes safety, citizenship, and employee training, with a commitment to community health and ethical practices. Competing with Fluor, Zachry stands out for its EPC (engineering, procurement, construction) expertise and safety standards. Recent initiatives include digital project management tools and carbon-neutral construction goals, aligning with sustainability trends.

+ ZACHRY GROUP [1 Press Release]

+ ZACHRY GROUP SELECTED FOR FEED SCOPE ON WORLD'S LARGEST LOW-CARBON BLUE METHANOL PROJECT

Zachry Group has been awarded the Front-End Engineering Design (FEED) scope for the Lake Charles Methanol II Project, a groundbreaking initiative that will establish the world's largest low-carbon "blue" methanol facility and rank as the fifth-largest methanol plant globally.

Located in Lake Charles, Louisiana, the plant is designed to capture approximately one million metric tons of CO₂ annually, setting a new standard in sustainable methanol production and contributing significantly to industrial decarbonization efforts.

The FEED phase, currently underway, will define the technical and economic foundations of the project and support its progression toward a Final Investment Decision (FID), which is anticipated in the second quarter of 2026.

"We're honored to partner with Lake Charles Methanol II on this transformative project," said John Zachry, Chairman and CEO of Zachry Group. "This award reinforces our leadership in executing technically advanced, low-carbon solutions at scale. Together, we're setting the standard for sustainability in industrial development."

Zachry Group's selection for the FEED scope reflects its proven expertise in delivering complex, first-of-a-kind energy infrastructure. With a legacy of innovation and a deep commitment to safety and sustainability, Zachry is uniquely positioned to help customers navigate the energy transition and meet evolving regulatory and environmental expectations.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ BECHTEL CORPORATION [3 Press Releases]

+ BECHTEL AND EIMISA PARTNER TO DELIVER MINING AND INFRASTRUCTURE PROJECTS IN CHILE

Collaboration brings together global EPC expertise and local construction capabilities to support complex project delivery Bechtel, a global leader in engineering, procurement, construction, and project management, today announced an agreement with Echeverria Izquierdo Montajes Industriales S.A. (EIMISA), a leading Chilean construction and industrial services company, to support the delivery of select large-scale mining and infrastructure projects in Chile and across South America.

As mining investment accelerates across the region, customers are placing greater emphasis on how complex projects are delivered - seeking efficient execution while maintaining high standards for safety and quality.

Under the agreement, Bechtel and EIMISA will work together on projects where their capabilities are most complementary, combining Bechtel's expertise in engineering, procurement, and project delivery with EIMISA's proven track record in direct-hire construction across Chile.

Source: Company Website

+ BECHTEL AND NABTU PARTNER TO ADVANCE NUCLEAR CONSTRUCTION APPRENTICESHIPS

Bechtel and North America's Building Trades Unions (NABTU) today announced a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) to advance and modernize apprenticeship programs supporting the construction of a new generation of U.S. nuclear power plants.

The agreement reflects a strategic alignment between one of the world's most experienced builders of nuclear infrastructure and North America's largest federation of skilled construction unions. Together, the organizations will strengthen apprenticeship pathways and expand specialized training to develop the craft expertise and quality standards required to deliver nuclear projects.

Through this partnership, Bechtel and NABTU will work together to ensure apprenticeship programs continue to evolve to meet the scale of resources needed as well as the technical requirements of modern nuclear construction.

Source: Company Website

+ BECHTEL AND TERRAPOWER BEGIN CONSTRUCTION AT NATRIUM® KEMMERER UNIT 1

Today, Bechtel, a global leader in engineering, procurement, and construction (EPC), serving as TerraPower's EPC partner, has begun mobilizing teams and transitioning into field execution at the Natrium® Kemmerer Unit 1 (KU1) site in Wyoming.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KIEWIT CORPORATION [3 Press Releases]

+ KIEWIT CORPORATION PROTECTING WHAT LIES BELOW

Near the base of Nch'kay', or Mount Garibaldi, the Squamish community, in western Canada, has lived for decades with the threat of large debris flows. These fast-moving surges of mud, water, trees and boulders can cascade down the river with little warning. The Cheekye River, whose name comes from the Skwxwu7mesh (Squamish Nation language) word for "muddy waters," has a long history of these events.

Because of the historical risk, development on the alluvial fan - the broad deposit of sediment formed where the river spreads out at the base of the mountain - had been restricted for years. Despite many existing homes and businesses on the fan being in harm's way, little had been done to reduce the risk. Sqomish Sea to Sky Developments, a partnership between the Squamish Nation and local land development company Matthews West, owns just over 500 acres of this land.

Source: Company Website

+ KIEWIT'S 85 YEARS IN CANADA

In 2026, Kiewit proudly marks its 85th year of building in Canada. The company's Canadian story began in 1941 in the North, supporting construction of the Norman Wells Oil Refinery and Pipeline. In 1949, Kiewit opened its first Canadian office in Vancouver, establishing a permanent presence in the country. From those early roots, the company expanded across regions and markets, building the relationships, workforce and operational capacity required to serve clients coast to coast to coast. For the past 85 years, Kiewit has delivered complex projects across transportation, energy, marine, water, industrial and emerging sectors that support a changing economy.

"Diversifying our markets has made us a stronger, more resilient business," said Ryan Tones, president of Peter Kiewit Sons ULC. "Our teams have built hydroelectric facilities, ports and marine infrastructure, highways and transit systems, pipelines and power facilities, and essential water and wastewater systems that protect communities and natural resources. Our investment in our Marine Yard on the West Coast and our continued growth in water, wastewater and mining, reflect our commitment to serving Canada's evolving infrastructure needs and creating long-term opportunities for our people and partners."

Today, Kiewit employs more than 4,000 people across Canada, including a strong and growing craft workforce. Positioned across the country, these teams are building on decades of experience while preparing for Canada's next chapter.

The following timeline highlights select milestones and projects from the past 85 years and illustrates Kiewit's enduring role in advancing Canada's energy, infrastructure and economic development.

Source: Company Website

+ KIEWIT CORPORATION TIME CAPSULE: RECORD-BREAKING BRIDGES

From bustling city centers to remote wilderness areas, bridges stand as testaments to human ingenuity and the relentless pursuit of connection. Over the years, Kiewit has contributed to several record-breaking bridges unlike any before their time. This collection of Kieways articles revisits those milestone projects.

Source: Company Website

SANTA CLARA, Calif., Dec. 9, 2025 - Just as individual market sectors are finding ways to move projects forward after a year of supply chain and cost turbulence, the ongoing labor shortage threatens to bog down construction unless owners and contractors embrace new approaches, according to the latest Market Conditions Report from DPR Construction, one of the nation's top technical builders.

+ PARSONS CORPORATION COMMON STOCK [3 Press Releases]

+ PARSONS CORPORATION PARSONS MARKS GROUNDBREAKING OF SR 400 EXPRESS LANES PROJECT IN GEORGIA

CHANTILLY, Va., April 27, 2026 -- Parsons Corporation (NYSE: PSN) joined the Georgia Department of Transportation (GDOT), the State Road and Tollway Authority (SRTA), project partners, and community leaders to mark the groundbreaking of the State Route 400 (SR 400) Express Lanes Project on April 22. SR 400 is the largest transportation infrastructure project in Georgias history and the largest transportation public-private partnership (P3) project in the United States.

Parsons serves as the lead designer on the SR 400 Peach Partners team, supporting delivery of the approximately $4.6 billion project. The project will add new, dynamically priced express lanes along a 16-mile corridor from the North Springs MARTA station in Fulton County to McFarland Parkway in Forsyth County, improving mobility and the regional economy, enhancing travel reliability, and supporting future transit opportunities in one of the regions most heavily traveled corridors.

This project represents a significant investment in the future of transportation in Georgia, said Mark Fialkowski, president of Infrastructure North America, Parsons Corporation. Parsons brings deep experience delivering complex, large-scale infrastructure programs, and we are proud to support this transformative project that will improve mobility, strengthen regional connectivity, and support long-term economic growth.

The SR 400 Express Lanes Project is a key component of Georgias Major Mobility Investment Program and is designed to address increasing congestion while providing reliable travel options for commuters and freight. The project will also enable future multimodal improvements, including transit enhancements along the corridor.

Parsons has supported critical infrastructure programs across Georgia for decades, providing engineering, design, and program management services that help modernize transportation systems and connect communities across the state. The companys role on the SR 400 project reflects its continued commitment to delivering innovative, resilient infrastructure solutions that meet the evolving needs of its clients.

Parsons has more than half a century of experience designing, delivering, and protecting the infrastructure that connects our communities around the world, including roads and highways; bridges; passenger and freight rail; public transit; airports; and ports and waterways. Our experience includes more than 10,000 miles of roadways, 4,500 bridges, and more than 50 advanced traffic management system deployments that help cities and states improve safety and travel efficiency while also reducing emissions and energy costs to enhance the quality of life in the communities we serve.

To learn more about Parsons road and highway expertise, visit www.Parsons.com/road-highway/.

About ParsonsParsons (NYSE: PSN) is a leading disruptive technology provider in the national security and global infrastructure markets, with capabiliti

+ PARSONS CORPORATION PARSONS DEMAND RESPONSE TECHNOLOGY TO OPTIMIZE AND ENABLE LOS ANGELES DEPARTMENT OF WATER & POWERS OPERATIONS AND STRATEGIC PLAN

CHANTILLY, Va., April 20, 2026 -- Parsons Corporation (NYSE: PSN) announced today that the company has been selected by the Los Angeles Department of Water & Power (LADWP) to provide demand response technology, integration, and services to help manage and optimize the operation of distributed energy sources within the electrical grid.

Utility providers around the world are reshaping their capacity plans to meet the growing needs driven by the boom of artificial intelligence and data centers, said Mark Fialkowski, president, Infrastructure North America for Parsons. We take great pride in helping cities around the world deliver resilient energy systems for their populations and look forward to applying our innovative technology to enhance LADWPs demand response and renewable electricity capacity.

Under the contract, Parsons will provide the Demand Response Management System (DRMS) to LADWP, which will serve as key infrastructure in the utilitys drive to achieve 100 percent clean electricity by 2035 or sooner, while helping expand LADWPs demand response capacity by more than fourfold under the five-year contract. Unlike traditional, siloed DRMS solutions, the Parsons system offers out-of-the-box interoperability in critical areas including meter data management (MDM), customer care, billing, Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition (SCADA) and automated distribution management systems (ADMS).

Parsons DRMS helps enhance grid resiliency by securely interfacing distributed energy resources with existing utility systems and legacy fuel supplies. By enabling seamless data exchange and coordination across DRMS, MDM, SCADA, ADMS, and other operational platforms, it empowers grid operators with improved situational awareness, control, and resiliency. Parsons has implemented meter and operational management integration solutions with more than 400 public power utilities across the United States.

To learn more about Parsons comprehensive solutions for distributed energy resource management, visit https://www.parsons.com/products/derms/.

About ParsonsParsons (NYSE: PSN) is a leading disruptive technology provider in the national security and global infrastructure markets, with capabilities across cyber and electronic warfare, space and missile defense, transportation, water and environment, urban development, and critical infrastructure protection. Please visit Parsons.com and follow us on LinkedIn to learn how were making an impact.

Media Contact:Bernadette Miller+1 980.253.9781Bernadette.Miller@parsons.com

Investor Relations Contact:Dave Spille+ 1 703.775.6191Dave.Spille@parsons.us

Source: West Corporation

+ PARSONS CORPORATION PARSONS SELECTED AS CONSTRUCTION MANAGER FOR MAJOR NEW YORK CITY TUNNEL PROGRAM

CHANTILLY, Va., April 14, 2026 -- Parsons Corporation (NYSE: PSN) announced today that the company has been selected by the New York City Department of Environmental Protection (DEP) to provide construction management services for the Newtown Creek Combined Sewer Overflow Storage Tunnel project in New York City. Parsons will serve as a member of Newtown Creek CSO Partners, a joint venture with AECOM and EPC Consultants Inc., supporting the delivery of a major underground infrastructure program designed to eliminate untreated sewer discharges into local waterways.

The 16-year program represents one of New York Citys most significant wastewater infrastructure investments, addressing longstanding environmental challenges across Brooklyn and Queens. Parsons will provide construction management (CM) services for the large-diameter storage tunnel, tunnel dewatering pump station, and other structures, drawing on its experience delivering complex tunnel and sewer programs in dense urban environments.

Parsons brings decades of experience delivering complex tunnel and water infrastructure programs that improve environmental outcomes and strengthen community resilience, said Mark Fialkowski, president, Infrastructure North America for Parsons. We are proud to support New York City on this critical project that will reduce sewer overflows, enhance water quality, and help meet important regulatory commitments.

The project, which includes approximately 3.25 miles of tunnel and related facilities, is critical to improving water quality and protecting public health in surrounding communities. Once complete, the system will capture and store excess combined sewer flows during storm events, reducing environmental impacts and supporting long-term sustainability goals. As the construction manager, Parsons will utilize the DEPs Construction Management Information System (CMIS), which the company originally helped develop and deploy, to support efficient project delivery, coordination, and oversight.

Parsons is a global leader in critical infrastructure excellence, with more than 80 years of experience planning, designing, and overseeing the construction of complex tunnel projects throughout the world, including the Rondout-West Branch Bypass Tunnel, Kensico-Eastview Connection Tunnel, and Catskill Aqueduct Repair and Rehabilitation projects; each of these signature NYCDEP projects, in which Parsons provided comprehensive CM services. With more than 250 tunnels and ocean outfalls around the world, Parsons projects span the full range of challenging geologic environments, from seismically active SouthernCalifornia to the Chesapeake Bays alluvial coastal plain to the mixed-face and hard-rock conditions found in and around New York City.

To learn more about Parsons water and wastewater expertise, visit https://www.parsons.com/markets/water-wastewater/.

About ParsonsParsons (NYSE: PSN) is a leading disruptive technology provider

urn:contentItem:6JJK-TSC3-RS8T-N043-00000-00ZACHRY GROUP - SECTOR NEWS: LEADERS & PEERS UPDATE2026-05-11T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:14:51ZLexisNexisSTOCKZACHRY GROUP - SECTOR NEWS: LEADERS & PEERS UPDATE

DASHBOARD: ZACHRY GROUP

Websitehttps://www.zachrygroup.com/IndustryConstrustion

ACTIVITIES:

Zachry Group, founded in 1924, is a leading construction and engineering firm headquartered in San Antonio, Texas. Employing over 20,000 people, it provides turnkey services in power, energy, chemicals, and industrial sectors, generating over $3 billion annually. Its subsidiaries, like JVIC, offer maintenance and fabrication. In 2024, Zachry expanded renewable energy projects, including solar and wind facilities. Its culture emphasizes safety, citizenship, and employee training, with a commitment to community health and ethical practices. Competing with Fluor, Zachry stands out for its EPC (engineering, procurement, construction) expertise and safety standards. Recent initiatives include digital project management tools and carbon-neutral construction goals, aligning with sustainability trends.

+ ZACHRY GROUP [1 Press Release]

+ ZACHRY GROUP SELECTED FOR FEED SCOPE ON WORLD'S LARGEST LOW-CARBON BLUE METHANOL PROJECT

Zachry Group has been awarded the Front-End Engineering Design (FEED) scope for the Lake Charles Methanol II Project, a groundbreaking initiative that will establish the world's largest low-carbon "blue" methanol facility and rank as the fifth-largest methanol plant globally.

Located in Lake Charles, Louisiana, the plant is designed to capture approximately one million metric tons of CO₂ annually, setting a new standard in sustainable methanol production and contributing significantly to industrial decarbonization efforts.

The FEED phase, currently underway, will define the technical and economic foundations of the project and support its progression toward a Final Investment Decision (FID), which is anticipated in the second quarter of 2026.

"We're honored to partner with Lake Charles Methanol II on this transformative project," said John Zachry, Chairman and CEO of Zachry Group. "This award reinforces our leadership in executing technically advanced, low-carbon solutions at scale. Together, we're setting the standard for sustainability in industrial development."

Zachry Group's selection for the FEED scope reflects its proven expertise in delivering complex, first-of-a-kind energy infrastructure. With a legacy of innovation and a deep commitment to safety and sustainability, Zachry is uniquely positioned to help customers navigate the energy transition and meet evolving regulatory and environmental expectations.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ BECHTEL CORPORATION [3 Press Releases]

+ BECHTEL AND TERRAPOWER BEGIN CONSTRUCTION AT NATRIUM® KEMMERER UNIT 1

Today, Bechtel, a global leader in engineering, procurement, and construction (EPC), serving as TerraPower's EPC partner, has begun mobilizing teams and transitioning into field execution at the Natrium® Kemmerer Unit 1 (KU1) site in Wyoming.

Source: Company Website

+ LUCY MARTIN NAMED PRESIDENT OF BECHTEL'S MINING & METALS BUSINESS

Bechtel today announced the appointment of Lucy Martin as president of its Mining & Metals (M&M) business, effective April 6, 2026. She succeeds Ailie MacAdam, who will retire in May after an exceptional 41-year career with the company.

Source: Company Website

+ BECHTEL SELECTED FOR PREPARATORY ENGINEERING ON OKLAHOMA ALUMINUM PROJECT

First new U.S. primary aluminum smelter in decades Bechtel has been selected by Emirates Global Aluminium (EGA) and Century Aluminum to perform preparatory engineering and early execution planning for a new primary aluminum smelter in Inola, Oklahoma - an investment expected to create thousands of jobs and support long-term economic growth in northeast Oklahoma.

When complete, the facility will be the first new primary aluminum smelter built in the U.S. since 1980 and is expected to double the current U.S. primary aluminum production, strengthening America's industrial base and supply chain resilience.

The project is expected to create approximately 1,000 permanent direct jobs and up to 4,000 construction jobs, delivering long-term workforce and community benefits across Rogers County and the greater region.

"Our team brings deep experience delivering complex U.S. megaprojects and aluminum facilities around the world," said Ailie MacAdam, President of Bechtel Mining & Metals. "We are proud to support EGA and Century as they advance this important investment in America's industrial future, and we look forward to working closely with our customers, partners, and the local community as the project moves into its next phase."

Source: Company Website

+ KIEWIT CORPORATION [3 Press Releases]

+ NNSA ANNOUNCES APPROVAL TO BEGIN FULL CONSTRUCTION OF SRS SPECIALIZED TRAINING FACILITY FOR PLUTONIUM PIT PRODUCTION VIEW ARTICLE

The U.S. Department of Energy's National Nuclear Security Administration (NNSA) has announced approval of start of construction and established a project baseline, known as Critical Decision (CD)-2/3 approval, for the High-Fidelity Training and Operations Center (HFTOC) at the Savannah River Site (SRS).

The HFTOC will mimic the capabilities in the Savannah River Plutonium Processing Facility (SRPPF) Main Process Building and allow operators to develop pit production competencies on "like-for-like" sets of equipment with surrogate materials. The capabilities at HFTOC are essential to develop SRS' pit production workforce and will expedite the production of war reserve plutonium pits once SRPPF comes online.

Source: Company Website

+ PROJET TRAMCITE SELECTS CONSTRUCTION KIEWIT CIE FOR UNDERGROUND UTILITY RELOCATION WORK

Projet TramCite has reached a new milestone with the selection of Kiewit, supported by CIMA+, to carry out underground utility relocation work on Rene-Levesque Boulevard and 1st Avenue in Quebec City. This work represents a key preparatory phase for the implementation of the future tramway.

Kiewit was selected following a rigorous and structured procurement process conducted in accordance with the highest industry standards. The company will rely on the technical expertise and execution capacity of its teams to deliver this complex scope of work. Active in Quebec since 1957, Kiewit has an established track record in the delivery of major infrastructure projects, including the reconstruction of the Turcot Interchange in Montreal, the extension of Highway 25, and the construction of hydroelectric facilities.

The underground utility relocation work will prepare two strategic downtown corridors for the future tramway. Particular attention will be paid to managing impacts and implementing mitigation measures for residents, businesses, and users of the area.

Source: Company Website

+ OGLETHORPE POWER SELECTS KIEWIT SUBSIDIARY FOR NEW MONROE COUNTY COMBINED-CYCLE PLANT

Tucker, GA (January 13, 2026) - Today, Oglethorpe Power announced it has reached another major milestone in the development of its new combined-cycle natural gas power plant in Monroe County, officially selecting Kiewit Corporation through its subsidiary, The Industrial Company (TIC), as the Engineering, Procurement and Construction (EPC) partner for the project. Owned by and serving 38 Electric Membership Cooperatives (EMCs) across Georgia, Oglethorpe Power is developing the new facility to support long-term, system-wide load growth across its cooperative membership, including homes, businesses and existing industries-not to serve any single customer or standalone project.

"We are proud to partner with Kiewit through its subsidiary TIC for this critical energy project that will support our mission to deliver safe, reliable and affordable energy to our Members and the millions of Georgians they serve," said Annalisa Bloodworth, Oglethorpe Power President & CEO. "Its proven success in delivering complex power projects safely, on schedule and within budget, combined with a commitment to safety and community, makes the company an ideal partner for this facility and aligns perfectly with our cooperative principles and dedication to Monroe County."

The TIC team is expected to mobilize in Spring 2026, with commercial operation of the new facility anticipated in 2029. Once online, the 1,425-megawatt plant will provide reliable, efficient electricity to homes and businesses across Georgia served by Oglethorpe Power's Member cooperatives.

Source: Company Website

+ PARSONS CORPORATION PARSONS MARKS GROUNDBREAKING OF SR 400 EXPRESS LANES PROJECT IN GEORGIA

CHANTILLY, Va., April 27, 2026 -- Parsons Corporation (NYSE: PSN) joined the Georgia Department of Transportation (GDOT), the State Road and Tollway Authority (SRTA), project partners, and community leaders to mark the groundbreaking of the State Route 400 (SR 400) Express Lanes Project on April 22. SR 400 is the largest transportation infrastructure project in Georgias history and the largest transportation public-private partnership (P3) project in the United States.

Parsons serves as the lead designer on the SR 400 Peach Partners team, supporting delivery of the approximately $4.6 billion project. The project will add new, dynamically priced express lanes along a 16-mile corridor from the North Springs MARTA station in Fulton County to McFarland Parkway in Forsyth County, improving mobility and the regional economy, enhancing travel reliability, and supporting future transit opportunities in one of the regions most heavily traveled corridors.

This project represents a significant investment in the future of transportation in Georgia, said Mark Fialkowski, president of Infrastructure North America, Parsons Corporation. Parsons brings deep experience delivering complex, large-scale infrastructure programs, and we are proud to support this transformative project that will improve mobility, strengthen regional connectivity, and support long-term economic growth.

The SR 400 Express Lanes Project is a key component of Georgias Major Mobility Investment Program and is designed to address increasing congestion while providing reliable travel options for commuters and freight. The project will also enable future multimodal improvements, including transit enhancements along the corridor.

Parsons has supported critical infrastructure programs across Georgia for decades, providing engineering, design, and program management services that help modernize transportation systems and connect communities across the state. The companys role on the SR 400 project reflects its continued commitment to delivering innovative, resilient infrastructure solutions that meet the evolving needs of its clients.

Parsons has more than half a century of experience designing, delivering, and protecting the infrastructure that connects our communities around the world, including roads and highways; bridges; passenger and freight rail; public transit; airports; and ports and waterways. Our experience includes more than 10,000 miles of roadways, 4,500 bridges, and more than 50 advanced traffic management system deployments that help cities and states improve safety and travel efficiency while also reducing emissions and energy costs to enhance the quality of life in the communities we serve.

To learn more about Parsons road and highway expertise, visit www.Parsons.com/road-highway/.

+ PARSONS CORPORATION PARSONS DEMAND RESPONSE TECHNOLOGY TO OPTIMIZE AND ENABLE LOS ANGELES DEPARTMENT OF WATER & POWERS OPERATIONS AND STRATEGIC PLAN

CHANTILLY, Va., April 20, 2026 -- Parsons Corporation (NYSE: PSN) announced today that the company has been selected by the Los Angeles Department of Water & Power (LADWP) to provide demand response technology, integration, and services to help manage and optimize the operation of distributed energy sources within the electrical grid.

Utility providers around the world are reshaping their capacity plans to meet the growing needs driven by the boom of artificial intelligence and data centers, said Mark Fialkowski, president, Infrastructure North America for Parsons. We take great pride in helping cities around the world deliver resilient energy systems for their populations and look forward to applying our innovative technology to enhance LADWPs demand response and renewable electricity capacity.

Under the contract, Parsons will provide the Demand Response Management System (DRMS) to LADWP, which will serve as key infrastructure in the utilitys drive to achieve 100 percent clean electricity by 2035 or sooner, while helping expand LADWPs demand response capacity by more than fourfold under the five-year contract. Unlike traditional, siloed DRMS solutions, the Parsons system offers out-of-the-box interoperability in critical areas including meter data management (MDM), customer care, billing, Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition (SCADA) and automated distribution management systems (ADMS).

Parsons DRMS helps enhance grid resiliency by securely interfacing distributed energy resources with existing utility systems and legacy fuel supplies. By enabling seamless data exchange and coordination across DRMS, MDM, SCADA, ADMS, and other operational platforms, it empowers grid operators with improved situational awareness, control, and resiliency. Parsons has implemented meter and operational management integration solutions with more than 400 public power utilities across the United States.

To learn more about Parsons comprehensive solutions for distributed energy resource management, visit https://www.parsons.com/products/derms/.

+ PARSONS CORPORATION PARSONS SELECTED AS CONSTRUCTION MANAGER FOR MAJOR NEW YORK CITY TUNNEL PROGRAM

CHANTILLY, Va., April 14, 2026 -- Parsons Corporation (NYSE: PSN) announced today that the company has been selected by the New York City Department of Environmental Protection (DEP) to provide construction management services for the Newtown Creek Combined Sewer Overflow Storage Tunnel project in New York City. Parsons will serve as a member of Newtown Creek CSO Partners, a joint venture with AECOM and EPC Consultants Inc., supporting the delivery of a major underground infrastructure program designed to eliminate untreated sewer discharges into local waterways.

The 16-year program represents one of New York Citys most significant wastewater infrastructure investments, addressing longstanding environmental challenges across Brooklyn and Queens. Parsons will provide construction management (CM) services for the large-diameter storage tunnel, tunnel dewatering pump station, and other structures, drawing on its experience delivering complex tunnel and sewer programs in dense urban environments.

Parsons brings decades of experience delivering complex tunnel and water infrastructure programs that improve environmental outcomes and strengthen community resilience, said Mark Fialkowski, president, Infrastructure North America for Parsons. We are proud to support New York City on this critical project that will reduce sewer overflows, enhance water quality, and help meet important regulatory commitments.

The project, which includes approximately 3.25 miles of tunnel and related facilities, is critical to improving water quality and protecting public health in surrounding communities. Once complete, the system will capture and store excess combined sewer flows during storm events, reducing environmental impacts and supporting long-term sustainability goals. As the construction manager, Parsons will utilize the DEPs Construction Management Information System (CMIS), which the company originally helped develop and deploy, to support efficient project delivery, coordination, and oversight.

Parsons is a global leader in critical infrastructure excellence, with more than 80 years of experience planning, designing, and overseeing the construction of complex tunnel projects throughout the world, including the Rondout-West Branch Bypass Tunnel, Kensico-Eastview Connection Tunnel, and Catskill Aqueduct Repair and Rehabilitation projects; each of these signature NYCDEP projects, in which Parsons provided comprehensive CM services. With more than 250 tunnels and ocean outfalls around the world, Parsons projects span the full range of challenging geologic environments, from seismically active SouthernCalifornia to the Chesapeake Bays alluvial coastal plain to the mixed-face and hard-rock conditions found in and around New York City.

To learn more about Parsons water and wastewater expertise, visit https://www.parsons.com/markets/water-wastewater/.
